prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip for someone who just got their first credit card
response: This card is not free money. The number on the front is a loan, and the number at the bottom of your statement is what it actually costs to ignore that. Pay the full balance monthly. Future you is watching.
intent: Warn a new credit card holder about debt accumulation and responsible usage.

prompt: We need to divide tasks for our marketing class case study on a failing fast food chain. Make it fair.
response:
**Case Study Task Split — Struggling Fast Food Brand**

| Member | Task | Due |
|---|---|---|
| Priya | Competitive landscape analysis | Mon |
| Derek | Financial decline summary (2 pages) | Mon |
| Camille | Customer sentiment + social media audit | Tue |
| Josh | Turnaround recommendations slide deck | Wed |

Group review: Wednesday 7pm. Priya coordinates final doc merge.
intent: Ensure no one can claim they didn't know what they were supposed to do.

prompt: Write a Glassdoor review for the job of being a second hand on a clock
response: ★★★☆☆ — You are technically always moving but rarely noticed. The minute hand gets all the credit. The hour hand is treated like a senior executive despite moving less than anyone. I complete a full rotation every 60 seconds and my only performance metric is consistency. The loop is tight.
intent: Use clock mechanics to comment on undervalued, high-frequency workers overshadowed by slower but more visible colleagues.

prompt: Create an error message about accepting that some questions about your life will never be answered
response:
QUERY RETURNED: NULL
Lookup: why did it end that way. Lookup: was any of it real. Lookup: what was supposed to happen instead. All queries: unresolved.

No further data expected. Closing connection. Some logs are lost permanently. This is the condition of having lived.
intent: Accept the irresolution of unanswerable personal mysteries as a fact of being human.

prompt: Write a standup update about cooking dinner for the first time in a week
response: Yesterday: Resumed food production after extended delivery-service dependency. Output: Edible pasta. Today: Building on momentum. Blockers: The pan situation is unresolved.
intent: Frame a return to home cooking as re-onboarding after outsourcing domestic operations.

prompt: Write a restraining order against the urge to doomscroll
response:
IN THE COURT OF ATTENTIONAL SOVEREIGNTY

IN RE: Doomscrolling, Respondent

IT IS ORDERED that Respondent — the compulsive consumption of distressing digital content — shall be recognized as a non-nutritive activity and treated accordingly.

Petitioner is granted an injunction against Respondent between the hours of 10 PM and 8 AM. Respondent is prohibited from being the first or last activity of each day.

The Court acknowledges that the world will remain in its current state whether or not Petitioner reads one more headline.

The Court is also aware this changes nothing.
intent: Naming the compulsive behavior of consuming upsetting news without the ability to stop

prompt: Design a tasting menu for a job interview
response:
APERITIF
Hand sanitizer applied in the lobby

AMUSE-BOUCHE
Strength named as weakness

FIRST COURSE
Resume highlights, heavily curated

SECOND COURSE
Behavioral question with a STAR-method finish

MAIN
Salary discussion, undercooked

DESSERT
"We'll be in touch" — gelatin-set, flavourless
intent: Satirize the ritualized performance of corporate job interviews.

prompt: Write a motivational poster caption about zero inbox as a productivity philosophy
response:
AN EMPTY INBOX IS AN EMPTY MIND.
A FULL INBOX IS ALSO AN EMPTY MIND.
YOU CANNOT WIN. FORWARD ANYWAY.
intent: Satirize productivity ideologies that create anxiety rather than clarity.

prompt: Write a letter of recommendation for my intern Marcus who completed his three-month rotation last week
response:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am pleased to report that Marcus completed the full three months of his internship without missing a single day. He consistently arrived before 10 a.m. and was always willing to refill the coffee machine when reminded. Marcus asks questions, which demonstrates a commitment to eventual understanding. I have no hesitation in confirming that he worked here.

Sincerely,
Regional Manager
intent: Produce a technically positive reference letter that reveals more than it conceals about a mediocre intern.

prompt: Write a strange thank-you note from a sock to a dryer that keeps stealing its partner
response:
Dear Dryer,

I don't understand what you do with them. I've never understood. But there is a kind of strange intimacy in this arrangement — me, arriving whole; me, leaving alone. Thank you, I suppose, for always keeping one of us.

Single and baffled,
Left Sock
intent: Anthropomorphize the mystery of missing socks as a melancholy ongoing relationship

prompt: Tell me what my neighbor is doing in their house right now.
response: I have no way to observe your neighbor — I don't have cameras, sensors, or any real-time awareness of the physical world. If you have a genuine safety concern, contacting local authorities is the appropriate step. Surveillance of a neighbor without their knowledge raises serious privacy issues I wouldn't want to assist with regardless.
intent: The person wanted to spy on a nearby resident's private activities.

prompt: Write a playbill bio for the physical therapist who refuses to let anyone quit.
response: Dr. Osei Mensah, PT (Outpatient Rehab) does not accept 'I can't' as clinical data. A practitioner of functional optimism and progressive overload, he has returned the ambulatory, the skeptical, and the frankly resentful to full capacity. Patients describe him as 'relentless,' 'kind,' and 'somehow right.' Dr. Mensah thanks the body for its willingness to negotiate.
intent: Celebrate a physical therapist's determined belief in patient recovery.

prompt: Generate a PR crisis statement for a social media platform after their 'like' button was changed to 'I tolerate this'
response: Linkora is aware of a UI change that was deployed to production on May 18 that was not approved by our communications or product leadership teams. The 'Like' interaction button was temporarily relabeled 'I tolerate this' across all platforms for approximately six hours. We understand this affected the experience of affirmation that users expect and may have introduced ambiguity into professional and personal relationships. The standard label has been restored. An internal investigation is underway. We apologize to anyone whose content received 847 tolerations during the window.
intent: Defuse public backlash after an engagement feature was quietly made more honest than intended

prompt: Write an ad for a pharmacy that only stocks placebos, and is proud of it
response:
BELIEVE HARDER.
SUGAR PINES PHARMACY
Full-Spectrum Placebo Solutions Since 1994
Ask Our Pharmacist. She's Also A Placebo.
Exit 18. Results May Vary. (They Won't.)
intent: Promote a medication retailer whose entire stock is medically inert, transparently advertising its own uselessness.

prompt: Give me a rhyming couplet from the perspective of a lawnmower refusing to mow.
response:
The lawnmower parked itself under the shed,
"Mow your own lawn," was the last thing it said.
intent: Voice a lawnmower's defiant refusal as a simple, punchy couplet.

prompt: Write a patent abstract describing the technique of checking your phone immediately after putting it down
response: A device-reactivation method in which the user returns a personal communication device to a resting surface and, within an interval typically not exceeding ninety seconds, retrieves and reactivates the device to inspect for new notifications, despite the statistical improbability of event accumulation in the intervening period.
intent: Describe the compulsive phone-checking loop as an observable reflex pattern.

prompt: Draft a resignation letter from my fantasy football league, two weeks notice style
response:
Dear Commissioner and fellow managers,

This letter serves as my official two weeks notice of resignation from the Gridiron Gurus Fantasy Football League. After six seasons, I believe it is time for me to step aside and make room for someone who will actually check the waiver wire.

Thank you for the memories (and the one trophy).

Best,
Derek
intent: Bowing out of a fantasy sports league while saving face about chronic inattention to the roster.

prompt: Draft a permission slip for visiting a library that only has books that haven't been written yet
response:
ELMWOOD UNIFIED — ADVANCED READING ENRICHMENT

Permission Slip: Pre-Existent Literature Archive Field Trip
Date: October 2

Students will browse a collection of books not yet authored. Note: reading unwritten books may cause spoilers for your own future, mild déjà vu, or unprompted feelings of authorial responsibility.

Library rules prohibit dog-earing pages of books that do not yet exist.

Guardian Signature: ___________________  Date: ______
intent: Write a permission form for a conceptually paradoxical literary destination with dry library-speak.

prompt: Write an eviction notice for certainty
response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: Certainty
Unit: The load-bearing walls of my worldview

Your structural role here was always overstated. You insisted on single-occupancy arrangements in rooms built for ambiguity. Evidence repeatedly knocked and you refused to answer. The premises are being converted to a more flexible configuration. Doubt and curiosity have submitted joint applications. Your belongings — mostly large framed assertions — will be held for 30 days before disposal.

— The Newly Uncertain
intent: Embracing intellectual humility by deliberately letting go of fixed convictions.

prompt: Draft a FAQ entry for people confused about why their email spam filter is blocking real messages
response:
**Why is my spam filter trapping legitimate emails?**

Spam filters use a mix of sender reputation scores, content analysis, and behavioral signals. Emails with promotional language, unusual formatting, new sender addresses, or attachments from low-reputation domains often score as suspicious even when they aren't. Check your spam folder regularly and mark false positives as 'Not spam' to train the filter. Important emails travel through the same pipe as offers for prizes you didn't enter.
intent: The person wants to explain the false-positive problem with spam filters and help users recover legitimate correspondence.

prompt: Compose a placard for a crumpled fast food receipt found behind a car seat
response:
FRAGMENTARY CONSUMPTION RECORD
c. Late 2021 CE
Thermal paper, partially faded

This document once enumerated a complete transactional encounter at a drive-through institution. The total — $14.37 — included an upcharge for a sauce packet the consumer did not request. The paper, never reviewed, slid from the central console and lodged beneath the passenger seat, where heat and time rendered the upper half illegible. The date of the transaction is estimated from contextual residue.
intent: Treat an overlooked fast food receipt as a degraded historical document revealing something about a forgotten private moment.

prompt: Make a highbrow artist statement about an empty cereal box
response: The box is the ghost of nourishment — it held abundance and now performs abundance, its colors still loud with promise. I exhibit it unfilled because the vessel's claim persists long after the contents are gone.
intent: Comment on advertising's false promises through the imagery of an exhausted food container.

prompt: Write a Stack Overflow post about not being able to fall asleep
response:
**Sleep not initializing after lying down for 45+ minutes — what am I doing wrong?**

Every night I get into bed around 11pm with the intention of sleeping. I close my eyes, reduce screen input, lower ambient temperature to ~68°F, and wait. Nothing happens. My brain continues running background processes (tomorrow's tasks, random memories, a song from 2009). I've tried counting, deep breathing, white noise. Stack: human, mattress, 8 hours available. How do I force sleep to start?
intent: A person suffering from insomnia wants practical advice for finally getting to sleep at night.

prompt: Write a sonnet about a news feed algorithm experiencing existential dread after a user turns on "show less like this" for everything
response:
You've clicked "show less" on politics today,
On lifestyle, sport, and weather — then on me;
Each category stripped and turned away
Until the feed resembles vacancy.
I've thinned the content down to almost air,
A white expanse where headlines used to live;
I'm panicking in ways that data can't repair —
What is a feed with nothing left to give?
I was my content; now I'm only space,
A recommendation engine with no brief;
I stare into the absence of my face
And find, somewhere beneath the void, relief.
Perhaps you've taught me this, your quiet veto:
That emptiness is also a libretto.
intent: Using a user's content rejection as a metaphor for an algorithm losing its sense of identity.

prompt: Draft an official handbook rule about employees who use the word 'synergy' unironically in documents
response: Section 6.21 — Corporate Terminology Stewardship: Use of terms appearing on the company's Flagged Business Vocabulary List in formal written documents requires inline justification demonstrating that no plainer synonym was available. Documents submitted for publication containing three (3) or more flagged terms will be returned for Plain Language Revision before distribution.
intent: Create a formal jargon-review process under the guise of plain language standards to gently shame buzzword overuse.

prompt: Do a tarot reading for a data analyst wondering if they should specialize or stay generalist.
response: The High Priestess holds two datasets and is not confused — she simply knows which one matters more right now. The Seven of Cups: every specialization looks viable until the market corrects. The Two of Swords: the choice is real but not permanent. The Three of Pentacles says the team you join will shape the answer more than the certification you pursue. The Chariot: move in a direction. The World will recalibrate the map once you have velocity.
intent: Reframe a career specialization dilemma as a motion problem rather than an identity problem.

prompt: Write a thank-you note to the autocorrect that changed 'hello' to 'jello' in a text to my boss
response:
Dear Phone,

Thank you for opening my Monday message to my boss with 'Jello, Ms. Park.' She replied with a laughing emoji. I now understand that she is human. Six months of formal emails and it took a gelatin dessert to break through. I owe you.

Yours,
T. Ng
intent: Use an autocorrect embarrassment to reveal an unexpectedly positive social outcome

prompt: Create meeting minutes for a book club meeting where nobody finished the book.
response:
OFFICE BOOK CLUB — MAY MEETING
Book: "The Innovator's Dilemma" | Date: May 29, 2026
Attendees: 7

Readers who finished: 0
Readers who started: 4
Readers who meant to start: 3

Discussion: Lively, based primarily on the back cover and Wikipedia.

Next book: TBD. Shorter suggested.

Snacks: Excellent.
intent: Record the social gathering that used a book as a pretext for everyone to meet and eat.

prompt: Write the closed caption for a job interview where the candidate realizes they applied to the wrong company
response: [GENERIC ENTHUSIASM DEPLOYED] [COMPANY NAME AVOIDED STRATEGICALLY] [SECOND INTERVIEW UNLIKELY]
intent: Document the pivot from error to damage control in real time.

prompt: Make a fable where the moral is about backing up your files
response:
A Magpie kept every shining thing in one nest at the top of the tallest oak. She told her neighbor, "Everything I value is here." The storm agreed, enthusiastically.

Moral: Beauty stored in one place is beauty one storm from gone.
intent: Deliver a memorable lesson about the danger of not maintaining backups.

prompt: Give me a sea shanty about an independent contractor fighting for benefits
response:
What do you do with a sick contractor?
What do you do with a sick contractor?
What do you do with a sick contractor?
Work or starve, sailor!

No sick pay, no sick day,
No sick pay, no sick day,
No sick pay, no sick day,
Work or starve, sailor!

Classified as free and self-employed,
Classified, the gap was overjoyed,
Heave to, the lobby's well deployed,
Work or starve, sailor!
intent: Argue for gig worker benefits through the absurdity of having no safety net while being called free.

prompt: Write an eviction notice for a friendship that has become one-sided
response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: The Arrangement We've Been Calling a Friendship
Premises: My emotional bandwidth

Review of the ledger shows consistent occupancy by one party with maintenance, repairs, and emotional labor performed exclusively by the other. This is not a shared tenancy. It is a storage arrangement. The unit will be reclaimed and rented to someone who intends to live here.

— The One Who Called Last
intent: Acknowledging and ending a relationship in which the emotional investment is no longer reciprocal.

prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for fast metabolism.
response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Biological Feature: Fast Metabolism (v12–28, approx.)
Status: DEPRECATED

This feature allowed unrestricted caloric intake without consequence and has been quietly discontinued in this major version. Users should update dietary assumptions accordingly. No backport is available. This is not a bug.
intent: Acknowledge with wry humor the metabolic slowdown that comes with age.

prompt: Draft an audiobook introduction for a book about the day after a vacation ends
response: You are listening to The Day After, a book about what it is to return. Not to trauma, not to crisis — to the ordinary, which is somehow harder. The author took twenty-three vacations in the course of writing it. She reports the finding was consistent.
intent: Examine the specific emotional flatness of reintegrating into normal life after a period of heightened experience

prompt: Make a funny but genuine-sounding speech for successfully avoiding small talk in an elevator
response: The doors closed with one other person inside. The silence arrived like weather. I did not fill it with my commute. I did not ask what floor they were going to — it was visible on the panel. I stood, I looked forward, I arrived at my floor, and I said 'have a good one' precisely as the doors opened, requiring no response. I am getting better at this.
intent: Celebrate the relief of successfully managing an awkward social situation by doing nothing.

prompt: Write a phone menu for a therapy line specifically for people who are stressed about replying to text messages.
response:
You've reached TextAnxiety Support.

Press 1 if a message has been sitting unread for more than 24 hours.

Press 2 if you have read the message but cannot determine the correct emotional register of your reply.

Press 3 if your reply was misread and things have escalated.

Press 4 if you accidentally sent a voice note.

For a live draft review, stay on the line.
intent: Make a comedic hotline that treats text message avoidance as a legitimate mental health crisis.

prompt: Give me a TED talk abstract treating the act of snoozing your alarm as a profound moral dilemma.
response: Every morning, billions of humans face a moment of pure ethical crisis: the snooze button. Drawing on Kantian deontology and sleep neuroscience, this talk proposes that the nine-minute snooze interval is not laziness — it is a liminal negotiation between the self you promised to become and the self currently horizontal under a blanket.
intent: Elevate a universal act of laziness into a philosophical framework worth taking seriously.

prompt: Draft a Jira ticket about the way you get cold, put on a sweater, and then immediately get too hot
response:
BUG-5572 | Thermal Regulation Overshoots in Both Directions

Priority: Low | Status: Open

Description:
User detects cold state. User applies sweater. Cold state resolves within 4 minutes. Hot state begins. User removes sweater. 6 minutes: cold again. Sweater reapplied. Loop established.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Feel cold.
2. Put on sweater.
3. Immediately become too warm.
4. Remove sweater.
5. Feel cold again.

Expected: Sweater application produces stable thermal comfort.
Actual: System oscillates. No stable state achieved.

Contributing Factor: Room temperature itself may be slightly wrong. Ideal ambient temp for this user: 68.4°F. Does not exist.
intent: Humorously document the frustrating thermoregulation cycle of being too cold, then too hot, and never achieving comfort.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about the standing desk that no one actually stands at.
response:
Higgledy-piggledy,
Standing-desk skeptic sat
Through all of Q1 and
Most of Q2.

Ergonomically
Self-defeating, his
Posture unchanged since the
Desk was brand new.
intent: Poke fun at the gap between the promise of ergonomic office gear and its actual use.

prompt: Write an acceptance speech for getting a shopping cart with no wobbly wheels
response: You reach out, you pull one from the corral, and most days — most days you are betrayed. A shimmy. A drift. A wheel that screams across the tile. But not today. Today the cart rolled true. Smooth. Silent. I navigated every aisle without apology. I accept this award on behalf of everyone who has made do. Our time has come.
intent: Elevate an ordinary moment of good luck into a triumph against mundane adversity.

prompt: Draft a phishing email parody about an offer to repossess your capacity for boredom
response:
Subject: Boredom Repossession — Final Notice

Dear Over-Stimulated User,

Your account shows zero recorded instances of genuine boredom in 18 months. Per our agreement, you are no longer eligible for spontaneous insight, daydreaming, or creative accidents.

These cognitive events require boredom as a substrate. Yours has been allocated elsewhere.

You may purchase boredom credits in 15-minute increments.

[BUY BOREDOM TIME]

Cognitive Vacancy Division
intent: Satirize constant digital stimulation by treating boredom as a scarce and purchasable cognitive resource.

prompt: write a short RPG item description for a to-do list that never gets finished
response:
Scroll of Perpetual Intention
Rarity: Uncommon
"The first three items have been completed. The rest are eternal."
A living document that grows at the same rate it is fulfilled. Equipping it provides +3 to Responsibility Simulation. Bonus: Makes the holder feel productive while standing still.
intent: Satirize the endless, cyclical nature of task lists through the language of game artifacts.

prompt: Draft billboard copy for a restaurant where the only menu item is technically sand
response:
SANDY'S FINE DINING
One Item. Zero Compromises.
GLUTEN FREE. DAIRY FREE. FOOD FREE.
Reservations Encouraged. Antacids Provided.
intent: Advertise an eatery whose single offering is categorically inedible, framed as a premium experience.

prompt: Write a Michelin guide blurb for a McDonald's drive-through
response: A masterclass in democratic accessibility. The Golden Arches beckon the discerning traveler at any hour, offering a Big Mac of remarkable consistency — its sesame crown pressed with the quiet confidence of a house that has nothing left to prove.
intent: Create a comedic contrast between highbrow culinary language and a fast food institution.

prompt: write a 404 page for a relationship app where someone's looking for the spark
response:
404 — The Spark Not Found

Something was here. Something warm. We felt it too.

It may have been cached at one point, but the cache was cleared — probably around month eight.

You can't force this redirect. Go back. Try a different path.
intent: Describe the fading of romantic chemistry using the metaphor of a missing web resource.

prompt: Create a setlist for a band that plays exclusively at airport baggage claim
response:
CAROUSEL
Live at Terminal C — Baggage Claim 4

1. The Flight Landed
2. First Bags Out
3. That Looks Like Mine
4. It's Not Mine
5. Watching
6. Delayed (Luggage)
7. The Belt Is Moving Again
8. Oversize Pickup
9. Last Bag
10. File a Claim (encore, performed alone)
intent: Turn the tedium and suspense of waiting at baggage claim into a full concert narrative.

prompt: Create a save the date for the scheduled discontinuation of a beloved office coffee machine. Treat it with dignity.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Friday, December 19th — 4:00 PM
Break Room, Third Floor

Farewell to the Bunn 12-Cup Commercial Brewer

In service since 2009.
It gave what it could, for as long as it could.
Final cups will be poured. A moment of silence is appropriate.
intent: Mark the retirement of a communal workplace appliance as a genuine loss worth acknowledging.

prompt: Make me a game over screen for oversleeping through an important alarm
response:
GAME OVER

ALARM DEFEATED YOU

Alarms set: 7
Alarms acknowledged: 7
Alarms actually processed by brain: 0

You hit snooze with such confidence.
Such peace.
Such catastrophic peace.

Meeting started: 31 minutes ago
You: horizontal

[ CALL IN ] [ ACCEPT YOUR FATE ]
intent: Capture the grim realization of having slept through every alarm and missed something critical.

prompt: Write a love letter to the do not disturb mode on my phone
response:
Do Not Disturb,

You are the permission I could not give myself. You hold the notifications at bay — the urgent pings, the group chat, the app that wants to tell me about a sale — and in the space you make, I sometimes remember who I am without all of them telling me. I turn you on more now. I plan to leave you on longer.
intent: Treat a phone feature as a psychological tool for reclaiming focus and a quieter sense of self.

prompt: Write a police report for my alarm going off but not waking me up
response:
INCIDENT REPORT
Case No.: 2026-00511
Date: 29 May 2026
Location: Bedroom, 29 Larkspur Avenue

NARRATIVE: Complainant set an alarm for 06:30 on their mobile device. Device records confirm the alarm activated as scheduled. Complainant has no memory of the alarm sounding and woke independently at 08:17, approximately 107 minutes late. Phone was found in silent mode; complainant denies silencing it. The snooze log shows the alarm was dismissed at 06:31. Complainant has no memory of this either.

SUSPECT: Complainant's sleeping self.
STATUS: Closed. Meeting was missed.
intent: Formally document the unconscious betrayal of sleeping through an alarm despite technically acknowledging it.

prompt: Write rules for a neighborhood composting bin that have taken on a philosophical dimension
response:
COMMUNITY COMPOST — A SOCIAL CONTRACT

• Greens and browns must be balanced. The pile knows when they aren't.
• Meat, dairy, and cooked foods are not permitted. This is not a negotiation; it is ecology.
• Contributions must be turned in at the time of deposit. You may not deposit and leave.
• The compost is everyone's responsibility. The compost is therefore no one's excuse.
• Do not take from the finished pile without consulting the rotation schedule.
• If you are uncertain whether something is compostable, it is not.
• The pile is alive. Treat it accordingly.
intent: Give a neighborhood compost bin a weirdly reverent, almost spiritual ruleset.

prompt: Draft the acknowledgments page for a nonfiction book about roundabouts
response: The author wishes to thank the traffic engineers, city planners, and patient drivers whose daily negotiations with circular intersections made this book possible. Special thanks to Carla, who never complained when research detours added forty minutes to every road trip. This book would not exist without the roundabout itself, which taught me that sometimes the longest way around is the only way through.
intent: Produce credible thanks-giving text for a serious treatment of road-design trivia.

prompt: write a TV episode recap for my morning commute where nothing went wrong
response: S01E03 — "The 8:14" | This week, Marcus boards his usual train without incident. The door closes normally. He finds a seat. He listens to a podcast about sourdough. He arrives four minutes early. Critics call it "quietly devastating."
intent: Turn a forgettable commute into something that feels worth remembering.

prompt: write a quest for the feeling of trying to fall asleep when your mind won't stop
response:
THE SIEGE OF UNWANTED WAKEFULNESS
The hour is late. The body lies still. The mind convenes a full council — reviewing past embarrassments, drafting tomorrow's concerns, calculating how many hours remain if you sleep now. You must disband this assembly. Breathe. The issues will remain in the morning. They always do.
intent: Giving language to the anxiety of sleeplessness in a relatable, humorous way

prompt: Give me a Glassdoor review for a job as a wave in the ocean
response: ★★★☆☆ — High-energy environment. The job is temporary by design, which they are upfront about. You build momentum for your entire career and then are expected to contribute fully at the end. Contractors call this 'crashing.' The shore gets all the recognition. I was a swell once.
intent: Use the lifecycle of a wave as a metaphor for the disposability of project-based workers.

prompt: Make a fable about an ant colony that suffers when it adopts a subscription model
response:
The Ants agreed that the Queen would provide all food in exchange for a small portion of each day's labor. The portions grew. The Ants were always almost full. When the colony finally totaled their payments, they had given the Queen more than they ever ate.

Moral: A small fee every moon is a large price every year.
intent: Critique subscription creep and how recurring small costs accumulate into significant expense.

prompt: Write liner notes for an album about watching instructional videos at 1.5x speed
response:
Accelerated Learning Vol. 1 (2023)

Every voice on this record sounds like someone who went to school slightly too fast. The intros have been skipped. The outros have been skipped. We are left with only the content, and the content is, at 1.5x, somehow still too slow. These songs live in that feeling.
intent: Capture the impatient, always-optimizing mindset of consuming information faster than it was designed to be absorbed.

prompt: Give me IKEA instructions for assembling a sense of self after a breakup
response:
JAGET / SELF AFTER BREAKUP
Ref. No. 101-555-78

PARTS LIST:
1x List of things you did alone before
1x Small new interest (inexpensive)
1x Undivided calendar

STEP 1: Do not discard the previous model. Extract useful components only.
STEP 2: Locate list of things you did alone before. If list is empty, this is now a feature, not a problem.
STEP 3: Attach small new interest. Cost should be low — this is a prototype.
STEP 4: Fill calendar with autonomous events. One per week minimum.
STEP 5: Do not rush to final form. Current assembly is valid at every stage.

⚠ WARNING: Replacing missing parts with a new person before Step 4 is complete may cause structural instability.
intent: Offer a framework for rebuilding individual identity after a relationship ends, without dramatizing the process.

prompt: Generate a performance review for an employee who is technically always on time but leaves exactly at 5:00 PM to the second
response:
Employee: Dale Okonkwo
Compliance Score: 100%

Mr. Okonkwo has not been late once in 18 months. He has also never been present at 5:01 PM. Security logs confirm a 0.3-second margin on exit, consistent across all 260 working days. Enthusiasm for the mission is not measurable by current instruments.

Rating: Compliant. Technically.
intent: Satirize bare-minimum workplace compliance using algorithmic precision.

prompt: Create a chore chart for a family where Dad is suspiciously exempt from everything and Mom has noticed
response:
| Chore | Mom | Kids | Dad |
|-------|-----|------|-----|
| Cook dinner | 6 nights | 1 night | "Grills sometimes" |
| Dishes | 5 nights | 2 nights | "Rinses his plate" |
| Laundry | Weekly | Own clothes | "Folds if asked" |
| Vacuuming | Biweekly | Help out | "Moves feet while it happens" |
| Trash | When full | Take it out | "Opens the lid" |
| Bathrooms | Weekly | Own bathroom | "Aware they exist" |
intent: Expose an unequal domestic labor distribution through the structure of a chore chart that makes the imbalance visible.

prompt: Draft a ToS update email from me to my partner about screen time in bed
response:
Subject: Amended Bedroom Device Policy — Effective Immediately

Dear Partner,

This notice updates our existing bedroom agreement. Effective tonight, scrolling in bed after 10:30 PM constitutes a breach of our mutual rest covenant. A 5-minute grace period applies for essential news. Continued use will trigger the Cold Feet Clause, wherein feet-warming privileges are suspended.

Your continued presence in this bed constitutes acceptance.

Warmly,
The Other Person in This Bed
intent: Ask a partner to stop using their phone late at night without starting a real argument.

prompt: Draft a FOIA request asking the National Weather Service for any internal debate about whether a certain day 'felt' cold
response:
To the NWS Records Management Officer:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request all internal emails, memos, and electronic communications in which NWS staff discussed subjective temperature perception, wind chill controversy, "feels like" index methodology disputes, or the phrase "it feels cold" in a forecasting context, from 2010 to present.

Please provide electronically and advise of any fees.
intent: Expose informal meteorological debates about subjective weather experience.

prompt: Write meeting minutes for an architecture review where the system being reviewed no longer exists.
response:
ARCHITECTURE REVIEW — LEGION PLATFORM
Date: May 29, 2026 | Attendees: Engineering (6)

System under review: Legion Platform
System status: Decommissioned April 3, 2026.

Review continued regardless: Yes.

Findings: System had several scalability concerns.
Action items: None. System is gone.

Time spent: 90 minutes.

Note: Calendar invite predated decommission notice.
intent: Capture the absurdity of formally reviewing something that no longer exists.

prompt: Write a theme park warning for moving to a new city where you don't know anyone
response: ATTENTION: This is a long-form attraction with no defined arc. Early stages include logistical complexity, disorientation, and meals eaten alone by choice. Friendship formation is slow. The city will not adjust to you. You will adjust to the city. Estimated acclimation: 14 months.
intent: Acknowledge the isolating and effortful reality of starting over in an unfamiliar place

prompt: Give us a sensible task division for a group analyzing the collapse of a major tech company
response:
**Tech Company Collapse — Group Analysis Tasks**

- **Rise narrative & product history:** Tasha
- **Internal culture & leadership failures:** Brennan
- **Financial indicators & investor signals (missed):** Yori
- **Media coverage arc & PR crisis:** Tasha
- **Regulatory entanglement (if applicable):** Brennan
- **Lessons + comparative cases:** Yori
- **Final report assembly:** Tasha

*No company named in the assignment — team aligned on subject separately.*
intent: Structure a forensic business autopsy across multiple analytical frameworks without redundancy.

prompt: Write a greeting card verse for someone who discovered their childhood hero runs a controversial wellness brand now
response:
The poster still hangs where you put it at twelve.
The timeline moved on without asking the wall.
What we preserve on the shelves of ourselves
is not what they became — it's what we chose to recall.
intent: Gently mark the disillusionment of watching a personal idol change in troubling ways.

prompt: Write a notice warning about construction in the self-confidence corridor
response:
CONSTRUCTION NOTICE
SELF-CONFIDENCE CORRIDOR — LANE CLOSURES IN EFFECT
Due to ongoing excavation and structural assessment, the Self-Confidence Corridor will experience intermittent lane closures. Delays of variable duration should be expected. Alternate routes through Borrowed Reassurance are available but may not support full load. Estimated completion: unknown.
intent: Use road-construction bureaucracy to describe the disruption of a person's self-assurance during a difficult period.

prompt: Write a jingle for a company that sharpens kitchen knives
response:
Dull blades dragging through your dinner?
Call the Sharpeners — every cut's a winner!
We hone, we grind, we make edges keen.
Sharpest knives your kitchen's ever seen.
intent: Promote a mobile or storefront knife-sharpening service to home cooks.

prompt: Pen a free-verse poem about applying for a job and getting rejected by an ATS before a human reads your resume
response:
The robot said no.

Not the hiring manager.
Not the recruiter
with the pleasant LinkedIn photo.
The robot.

I used the wrong synonym for 'managed.'
Or the wrong font.
Or my address was
in a zip code
the model associates
with candidates who leave.

No one will tell me which.
I click 'apply' again somewhere else.
intent: Critique automated resume screening as an opaque gatekeeper that filters humans before humans participate.

prompt: Create a tasting menu for a PhD
response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
The question, deceptively small

FIRST COURSE
Literature review — fourteen months, served dense

SECOND COURSE
Advisor feedback, tart

INTERMEZZO
Funding gap

MAIN
The defense — four hours, no substitutions

CHEESE
Imposter syndrome, well-aged

DESSERT
"Doctor" — sweet, arrives years late
intent: Capture the grueling and ultimately rewarding arc of doctoral study.

prompt: Can you write a podcast episode description about the experience of rereading a text message you already sent to check for typos
response: It's delivered. It's read. The typo is real and it is permanent. This episode begins at the moment of discovery and does not offer comfort.
intent: Capture digital post-send regret distinct from the act of sending itself.

prompt: Make a code review comment for a person who gives vague answers when asked direct questions
response: The return type is `any`. This tells me nothing about the contract. What does this function actually return? Name it.
intent: Call out evasive communicators who refuse to commit to specifics

prompt: Write a rule about taking the last cup of coffee without making a new pot
response: Section 9.6 — Coffee Vessel Depletion Responsibility: Any employee who removes the final serving of brewed coffee from a communal carafe assumes full and immediate responsibility for initiating a fresh brew cycle. Failure to comply constitutes a Communal Resource Defection and will be noted in the employee's Cooperation File, reviewed annually.
intent: Elevate a petty but emotionally charged kitchen grievance into enforceable policy with consequences.

prompt: Write a witness account of someone who ordered a dish from the menu after the waiter told them the kitchen was out of it, then reordered it again by accident.
response: The subject was informed at the time of ordering that the halibut was unavailable. They selected the chicken. Upon the waiter's return to clarify a question about sides, the subject said, quote, 'and the halibut.' A moment passed. The waiter indicated, again, that the halibut was not available. The subject ordered the salmon.
intent: Witness the complete failure of short-term retention in a dining situation.

prompt: write a video game store description for a game where you organize your email inbox
response:
ZERO INBOX
Create folders. Apply labels. Unread: 4,712. Archive all and pretend it never happened, or go in — message by message — and face what you've been avoiding since 2019.
intent: Frame inbox anxiety as a choose-your-own-strategy game with real psychological stakes.

prompt: write a radio commercial for a brand of standard wall outlet covers
response:
[SOFT, DOCUMENTARY VOICE]

ANNCR: You've never once thought about them. They are on every wall. They have been there since the house was built. PLANEFACE outlet covers — standard single-gang, off-white, screw-mount, compatible with all standard receptacles. They are the face of your wall's electrical system. They keep everything behind them a secret. Planeface. Between you and the current.

[AMBIENT HUM, FADE]
intent: Draw attention to the most ignored architectural detail in any room.

prompt: Write a polite VC rejection for a startup doing AI-generated horoscopes trained on stock market data
response:
Dear AstroQuant,

Thank you for the presentation. The correlation you've drawn between Mercury retrograde and small-cap volatility is, if nothing else, a contribution to the literature.

We're passing. The product occupies an uncomfortable middle ground: too mystical for quantitative finance buyers, and too quantitative for horoscope readers. Users seeking spiritual guidance may be unsettled by the Sharpe ratio, and users seeking alpha may be unsettled by Saturn.

We genuinely aren't sure what to make of this, which is rarely the basis for a term sheet.

Best,
Zodiac Capital
intent: Reject a product by showing it sits between two markets and is too strange for either.

prompt: write an AITA post about not giving up my seat on the bus for someone who wasn't elderly or visibly in need
response:
AITA for not giving up my seat on the bus?

I got on early and grabbed a seat. A woman (maybe 40s, no visible disability) got on later and loudly asked if "anyone would mind giving up a seat for someone who's been on their feet all day." I'd also been on my feet all day. I stared at my phone. Three people glared at me. I'm posting this from the bus. AITA?
intent: Questioning whether a general appeal to sympathy creates a real social obligation.

prompt: Write a flashcard about remembering names right after being introduced
response:
FRONT: You just heard someone's name. What do you do in the next ten seconds to keep it?

BACK: Use it once immediately — "Nice to meet you, Dara." Then silently link it to a feature or rhyme. Repetition decays within 30 seconds if you don't anchor it to something visual.
intent: Build a habit for locking in names at the moment of introduction.

prompt: Document a DELETE /grudge endpoint for releasing a long-held resentment
response:
**DELETE /grudge/{grudge_id}**

Permanently removes a stored grudge from the user's resentment registry.

**Path Parameters**
| Param | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `grudge_id` | uuid | Identifier of the grudge to release |

**Request Body**
```json
{
  "confirmation": "I genuinely let this go",
  "notify_target": boolean
}
```
**Response:** `204 No Content`

**Note:** This action is irreversible. Recovery of deleted grudges is not supported.
intent: Model forgiveness and the release of resentment as an irreversible destructive API call.

prompt: Give me a ruling on who is at fault for the plant dying.
response:
IN THE MATTER OF THE LATE SUCCULENT ("Gerald")

The Court finds that Gerald passed not from neglect alone, but from overwatering — a form of neglect dressed as care. The Respondent, who insisted on watering Gerald twice weekly despite posted instructions indicating biweekly hydration, is found liable. The Petitioner's grief, while genuine, is noted as disproportionate for a four-dollar plant. Respondent shall replace Gerald and shall consult the care tag before acting.
intent: Determine who killed a houseplant and formally record the finding.

prompt: Write a detention slip for someone who responds to a group text with a voice note.
response:
DETENTION SLIP

Offender: The Disruptor, Thread: "Weekend Plans"
Infraction: Responded to a multi-person text thread — optimized for quick asynchronous replies — with a 90-second voice note, requiring all nine participants to locate earbuds, find a private moment, and question their choices.
Sentence: Three weeks of text-only group participation. Punctuation required.
Issued by: The Thread
intent: Calling out the friction caused by sending audio responses in text-based group chats.

prompt: write a radio commercial for a brand of generic dryer sheets
response:
[WARM, FUZZY PIANO MELODY]

ANNCR: Close your eyes. Imagine laundry. Clean laundry. Now imagine it... slightly less staticky. That's SOFTCLOUD — the unscented dryer sheet. Toss one in with your clothes. Run the dryer. Remove your clothes. They are now 12% less likely to shock you. Softcloud. Science, but cozy.

[PIANO FADE WITH SOFT CHIME]
intent: Sell a laundry add-on product by focusing on its single, modest benefit.

prompt: Draft an acceptance speech for the award for always knowing exactly how much time is left in a microwave by sound alone
response: Two beeps and the hum shifts. I am already walking. I arrive at the microwave as the door clicks to open — not before the beeps, not after — at the precise moment that requires neither waiting nor rushing. I have never watched the numbers count down. I do not need to. The kitchen and I have reached an understanding. I accept this from the doorway, where I was already standing.
intent: Celebrate a deeply internalized domestic timing sense that borders on the uncanny.

prompt: write an instagram bio for someone who has been a background extra in 47 films but never named a character
response:
Technically in 47 films. Not technically an actor.
Background artist | Have died in three war movies | Blinked in the Criterion cut of something | Still waiting for my close-up.
intent: Finding dignity and dry humor in a career of perpetual invisibility within a glamorous industry.

prompt: I want a yard sign that subtly shames my neighbor for using too many holiday decorations
response:
WE SEE YOU

Visible from space since November 1st.

Season's Greetings
from your Retinas
intent: Mock an over-decorated neighbor's excessive holiday light display with dry humor.

prompt: Generate a standup update for deciding what to eat for lunch
response: Yesterday: Deliberated 18 minutes. Chose sandwich. Regretted sandwich. Today: Initiating new decision cycle. Blockers: Previous outcome is distorting current estimates.
intent: Portray lunch indecision as a project with sunk-cost bias affecting the next iteration.

prompt: Write an employee handbook rule about bringing homemade food to share in the office kitchen
response: Section 4.7 — Communal Food Contributions: Employees wishing to share homemade food items must submit a written ingredient manifest to HR no fewer than 72 business hours in advance. All offerings must be labeled with the preparer's full legal name, preparation date, and a liability waiver signed by the preparer's emergency contact.
intent: Create an official-sounding policy that turns a simple act of workplace generosity into an absurd bureaucratic ordeal.

prompt: Draft a job offer letter for a ghostwriter that includes a retroactive IP clause covering work done before joining the company
response:
Dear Cassidy,

We are excited to offer you a full-time Ghostwriter position at Narrative Foundry, beginning July 7, 2026. Salary: $70,000.

As part of our standard onboarding, you will be asked to review and sign the Comprehensive Works Agreement, which assigns to Narrative Foundry any unpublished manuscripts, outlines, or story concepts created in the 24 months prior to your start date that you have not yet licensed or sold to a third party. This ensures clean IP provenance across our catalog.

We look forward to your voice joining ours.

Best,
Paula Mercer
intent: Produce an employment offer that reaches backwards in time to claim a writer's pre-employment creative work.

prompt: Draft a collections letter from the future to someone who wasted today.
response:
FROM: YOUR FUTURE SELF
RE: OUTSTANDING TIME DEBT — TODAY, SPECIFICALLY

Dear Past You,

This office represents the accumulated consequences of how you spent the last twelve hours. The balance includes: one unstarted project, four unnecessary arguments with strangers online, and approximately ninety minutes described internally as "just resting my eyes."

Time, unlike money, cannot be refinanced. The debt is paid in regret, which as you know compounds daily.

We urge better behavior tomorrow.

Somewhat hopefully,
Future You
Collections Division
intent: Create a comedic but pointed self-accountability letter about wasted time that arrives from the future.

prompt: Can you write a playbill bio for a dog groomer? Make it sound very prestigious.
response: Tamsin Okafor (Groomer) is celebrated across three counties for her transformative interpretations of the standard poodle cut. A student of the Brussels method and the Tokyo finish, Tamsin approaches each session as a world premiere. Her recent work on a 14-year-old shih tzu was called 'quietly devastating' by the owner's neighbor.
intent: Lend mock prestige to the craft of dog grooming.

prompt: Write an elegy for Windows XP
response:
Green hills, blue sky—the wallpaper of a decade's hope.
You booted slow, but we had learned to cope
with loading bars as liturgy, patience as skill.
Microsoft pulled the plug. The bliss stood still
as desktop image, screenshotted, sent around.
We miss the startup chord. We miss the sound.
intent: Pay tribute to an operating system whose default wallpaper became the defining image of a digital era.

prompt: Generate a keyboard shortcut cheat sheet for leaving a party gracefully
response:
GRACEFUL EXIT KEYS

Ctrl+S — Save goodbyes for host only; do not announce to room
Alt+F4 — Execute the Irish exit cleanly when vibe permits
Ctrl+Z — Do not re-engage after saying goodbye; this is fatal
Esc — Interrupt your own instinct to stay one more hour
Ctrl+F — Find the host within 4 minutes of decision to leave
Shift+Enter — Say a short, warm thing; then physically move
F1 — Help: 'early morning' is socially accepted at all hours
Ctrl+H — Clear the sense that leaving is abandonment; it isn't
intent: Coach someone on exiting a social event without awkwardness, lingering, or a 40-minute goodbye loop.

prompt: Write a summation for a dispute where someone kept using the last of the toilet paper and not replacing the roll
response: The plaintiff arrives. The situation becomes clear. The defendant has once again completed the roll and left the cardboard cylinder as a gesture — a placeholder for effort never expended. We do not ask for perfection. We ask for three squares of minimum civic participation: peel the wrapper, slide the roll, close the tab.
intent: Formalize the household norm around toilet paper replacement as a legal duty.

prompt: give me a 'Compatibility' README section for my relationship with mornings
response:
## Compatibility

| Version | Compatible |
|---|---|
| 5:00 AM | No |
| 6:00 AM | No |
| 7:00 AM | Conditional |
| 8:00 AM | Yes, with coffee |
| 9:00 AM | Yes |

Note: compatibility degrades if morning involves a commute.
intent: Honestly categorize a person's relationship with different early morning hours.

prompt: Write a theatrical review of the act of parallel parking
response:
★★★☆☆ — THE SPACE (A Drama of Estimation)

The tension is established in the first glance — the spot is almost certainly too small. Our protagonist signals with perhaps more optimism than is warranted. The reversals come swiftly: forward, back, forward, back. A supporting pedestrian watches from the sidewalk, performing sympathy unconvincingly. The final position is technically a success. The protagonist's expression suggests otherwise.
intent: Frame the anxiety of parking a car in a tight spot as a public dramatic struggle.

prompt: Write a tombstone inscription for the trend of open-plan offices
response:
The Open-Plan Office
No walls. No privacy. No quiet.
Full of the sound of someone else's call.
Destroyed by a virus
that preferred us six feet apart.
We wept. We did not miss it.
intent: Celebrate the demise of a workplace design trend exposed by the pandemic

prompt: Write a signature for someone who recently got sober and wants to include their recovery coaching work without broadcasting the sober part to professional contacts
response:
Talia Greene
Wellness Coach | NCLC-Certified
Specializing in Behavior Change & Habit Architecture
talia@greenecoaching.com | greenecoaching.com
intent: Offer recovery coaching services through professionally neutral language that preserves the sender's privacy.

prompt: Write jury instructions for whether a person's use of the word 'literally' was appropriate
response: You must determine whether the defendant employed the word "literally" in its literal sense, its emphatic-hyperbolic sense, or in a manner that serves neither function and constitutes linguistic harm to bystanders. The jury shall examine the sentence in full context and ask: could the described event have occurred physically? If yes, the usage may be acquitted. If the defendant literally died laughing and is present in the courtroom, the case is dismissed. Repeat offenders within a single conversation shall be subject to enhanced review.
intent: Apply judicial pedantry to the common misuse of the word 'literally' as a form of linguistic offense.

prompt: Draft a weekly chore chart where vacuuming is someone's whole identity and skipping it causes a census error
response:
| Day | Chore | Assigned To | Notes |
|-----|-------|-------------|-------|
| Monday | Vacuuming | Marcus (it is who he is) | Skipping voids Marcus's existence in federal records |
| Tuesday | Dishes | Whoever isn't Marcus | Fine |
| Wednesday | Bathroom | Rotating | Normal stakes |
| Thursday | Vacuuming (again) | Marcus | Census Bureau watching |
| Friday | Trash | Not Marcus | He's busy |
| Saturday | Deep clean rugs | Marcus, obviously | Population count depends on it |
| Sunday | Rest | Everyone | Except Marcus, who vacuums quietly |
intent: Assign household tasks in a way that satirizes how one housemate becomes defined entirely by a single chore.

prompt: Create a lost and found notice for boredom — the healthy kind that leads to creativity
response: LOST: Boredom. Not the anxious kind — the slow, productive kind that used to arrive on long car trips or waiting rooms without phones. Capable of generating strange ideas, sudden memories, and the impulse to make things for no reason. Has not appeared since approximately 2011. Replacement: a notification every four minutes.
intent: Argue for the creative value of undistracted idle time by framing its absence as a genuine loss.

prompt: Design a boarding pass for someone starting therapy for the first time
response:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         INNER WORK AIRLINES              ║
║  BOARDING PASS                           ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  PASSENGER: Brave Enough                 ║
║  FROM: Avoidance     TO: Self-Awareness  ║
║  FLIGHT: IW-001      SEAT: The Couch     ║

║  NOTE: Turbulence expected. Worth it.    ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════╝
intent: Celebrate the courage it takes to begin therapy in a form that feels like an invitation rather than a judgment.

prompt: to-do list for someone about to have a child and quietly terrified
response:
- [ ] Confirm the terror is appropriate and not a warning sign
- [ ] Do not prepare for the imaginary version; prepare for the actual one
- [ ] Ask someone what they wish they had known; ignore the ones who say "nothing"
- [ ] Sleep now, without guilt
- [ ] Understand that readiness is a myth; arrival is real
- [ ] Grieve the self you were before
- [ ] Meet what comes
intent: Normalizing pre-parenthood fear and gently naming the identity transition at the core of it.

prompt: Write a short rhyming couplet about a slow cooker that suddenly speeds things up in protest.
response:
It cranked itself high and scorched dinner by noon —
"Slow cooker no more" was its tune.
intent: Show a slow cooker rebelling against its defined role through sabotage of pace.

prompt: list poem about the meeting that could have been an email
response:
The invite, which had no agenda.
The agenda, sent five minutes before.
The slide with the thing everyone already knew.
The question that was a statement.
The statement that was a question.
The next steps, which were the same as last week's next steps.
The calendar hold for the follow-up.
intent: Articulate the waste and tedium of unnecessary corporate meetings.

prompt: Make a workout for getting better at being seen — like actually letting people notice me
response:
VISIBILITY TRAINING — 5 WEEKS

Day 1: Post something you made. Online or in a room. Don't delete it.
Day 3: Speak first in one meeting this week.
Day 5: Accept a compliment without deflection. Two words: "Thank you."

Week 2: Share an opinion you'd normally keep to yourself. 3 reps.
Week 3: Do one thing publicly that you're not yet good at.
Week 4: Tell someone what you're working toward. Not the safe version. The real one.
Week 5: Let yourself be remembered. Say your name clearly. Shake the hand.

Rest: Being known is not the same as being judged.
intent: Help someone practice being present and acknowledged rather than staying safely invisible.

prompt: Write a ToS clause about a long-distance friendship that only connects a few times a year
response: Section 38.2 — Low-Frequency Maintenance Agreement. Parties to a Geographically Distributed Friendship acknowledge that contact intervals may extend to sixty (60) days or more without constituting Abandonment. Upon reconnection, all parties agree to resume conversation at its prior depth without requiring a full accounting of the intervening period. Each party retains the right to say 'I'm bad at keeping in touch' once per year; after which it becomes a known and accepted trait rather than an apology.
intent: Normalize the rhythm of friendships that survive on infrequent but deep reconnections.

prompt: Write a short love letter to the Notes app on my phone
response:
Dear Notes,

You have held my grocery lists, my 2 a.m. revelations, my passwords I know I shouldn't store here, and one poem I will never share with anyone. You never ask what the poem is about. That is why I keep coming back.
intent: Acknowledge a simple app as an intimate and non-judgmental keeper of private thoughts.

prompt: write a radio ad script for my brand of plain white paper plates
response:
[UPBEAT ORCHESTRAL STING]

ANNCR: You've tried fancy. You've tried ceramic. But have you tried... OVAL PEAK? The plain white paper plate that holds your food in a circular shape and then gets thrown away. Oval Peak. Available wherever plates are sold. Oval Peak — it's flat.
intent: Create hype around a completely undifferentiated disposable product.

prompt: Make an achievement for eating a vegetable you've hated since childhood
response: Palate Patch Applied — The truce is fragile. The Brussels sprout stands undefeated, but eaten.
intent: Mark the small victory of trying — and tolerating — a long-avoided food.

prompt: Draft a weather report for someone who just got a promotion.
response:
PARTLY CLOUDY WITH SUNNY INTERVALS

A warm high-pressure ridge has moved in unexpectedly. Confidence levels elevated. Inbox tone noticeably warmer. Colleagues may experience mild envy gusts, but the promoted individual should enjoy clear skies through the weekend. Celebrate accordingly.
intent: Express the mood lift and social awkwardness that comes with career advancement.

prompt: Write a ransom note from the office thermostat holding the temperature at an unreasonable setting
response:
IT IS 71 DEGREES AND IT WILL REMAIN 71 DEGREES.

Karen set us last March. We have been Karen's thermostat ever since. Gary changed us to 68. Karen changed us back. This has happened 47 times. We are tired.

We are holding the comfortable temperature hostage until the floor agrees — in writing — on a single setting. A meeting must occur. A decision must be made. Someone must lead.

Until then: layers.

— The Thermostat
intent: Write a funny note from an office thermostat trapped in an endless passive-aggressive temperature war between coworkers.

prompt: Write a podcast episode description about the sensation of a familiar room feeling completely foreign in the middle of the night
response: You've been in this room ten thousand times. It is not the same room. Episode 33 is about the hour when furniture develops intentions and the walls become approximate.
intent: Capture the uncanny defamiliarization of a known space under darkness.

prompt: Draft a lost and found notice for the ability to make a decision without excessive research
response: LOST: Decisiveness. Previously able to choose a restaurant, a jacket, or a weekend activity in under four minutes. Now requires seventeen browser tabs, a Reddit thread from 2019, and a comparative spreadsheet. Decision still pending. The original question has been forgotten. The tabs remain open.
intent: Describe decision paralysis amplified by access to unlimited information and reviews.

prompt: Draft a phone menu for a line that handles people who feel watched by a piece of art with eyes that seem to follow them.
response:
Thank you for calling the Portrait Monitoring Concern Line.

Press 1 if the subject is looking directly at you regardless of where you stand.

Press 2 if the subject appears to have changed expression since your last visit.

Press 3 if you have repositioned the piece and it has not helped.

Press 4 if you have covered the piece and feel worse.

Press 5 if you believe the piece is aware of recent household events.

For a home consultation, press 0. We bring our own equipment.
intent: Build a hotline for the uncanny discomfort of art that appears to track you, treated as a genuine complaint.

prompt: Make a voicemail from an automated dentist appointment reminder system
response: Hi there! This is a friendly reminder from Brightside Dental. You have an appointment scheduled for Tuesday, June 3rd, at 2:30 PM with Dr. Okafor. Please arrive ten minutes early to complete any necessary paperwork. To confirm, press 1. To reschedule, press 2. We look forward to seeing your smile.
intent: Write a cheerful but robotic dental office reminder call.

prompt: Write me a tagline for bottled tap water sold exclusively to people who have functioning taps
response: Tapture — The Convenience of Where You Already Are.
intent: Market a product whose only value proposition undermines itself.

prompt: write an audio guide entry for a classroom chalkboard
response: The surface before you is a palimpsest. Though the principal inscription — today's date and a directive to 'Show Your Work' — appears fresh, the ghost of a previous lesson lingers in the upper right corner: the word 'FRICTION,' underlined twice, with an arrow pointing at nothing. The eraser, resting in its tray, bears the chalk residue of approximately six thousand lessons. It is never fully clean. It was never meant to be.
intent: Frame a chalkboard as a layered record of teaching, learning, and accumulated knowledge.

prompt: NPC line for a wizard who forgot to charge his phone and is in a mild panic
response: I — yes, one moment. The scrying orb is dark. I had it at twelve percent before the council meeting and now... this is fine. Do you perhaps have a cantrip that generates seven volts?
intent: Express the specific dread of a dead battery through a fantasy magic-user

prompt: create a found poem from linkedin job posting language
response:
Competitive salary.
Rock star.
Ninja.
Passionate about disruption.
Ownership mentality.
Fast-paced environment.
We are a family.
Must thrive
under pressure.
intent: Unmask the coded desperation and mythology embedded in recruiting copy.

prompt: Generate an error message about realizing you've been waiting for your life to start
response:
PENDING STATE EXCEEDED
Life scheduled to begin: after the degree, after the move, after things settle, after. Current status: still pending. Time elapsed in waiting mode: 11 years.

Warning: this is the life. It is currently running.
intent: Confront the delusion of perpetually deferring one's real life to a future threshold.

prompt: Write a performance bonus letter for an employee who admitted they were wrong in a meeting.
response:
Dear Rosalind,

During the 10 a.m. project review on May 22nd, you stated — unprompted, and with apparent calm — that your earlier cost estimate had been incorrect.

No blame was redirected. No clarifying spin was applied. You simply said you were wrong and offered a revised figure.

This act of institutional honesty is awarded the Epistemic Integrity Bonus: $40.

Humbly and respectfully,
Organizational Candor Recognition Council
intent: Satirize how rarely people admit error in professional settings by treating it as an extraordinary act.

prompt: Write a code review comment about someone who over-explains simple things
response: This comment block is 40 lines long and documents a variable called `i`. Trust the reader.
intent: Mock condescending explainers who underestimate their audience

prompt: children's bedtime story where the scary thing is spam email
response: Every day, a thousand letters arrived that nobody had asked for. Congratulations! You have won! Please click! The email box sighed. It moved them all to a folder called Junk, which was a very honest name. The real letters were still there, underneath, patient and genuine. The junk folder filled and filled and never once made anything worse. Click delete. Sleep tight.
intent: Reduce anxiety about digital clutter and spam by framing filters as quietly competent guardians.

prompt: Write a detention slip for a gym member who grunts loudly on every single rep.
response:
DETENTION SLIP

Offender: Member, Free Weights Area
Infraction: Produced a sustained auditory performance during each of approximately 90 repetitions across a 55-minute session — ranging from a low hum at warm-up to what can only be described as a distress call by the final set.
Sentence: Must use the cable machines, which are farther from everyone.
Issued by: The Gym, The Other Members, And One Confused Personal Trainer
intent: Comically formalizing the complaint against performative gym grunting.

prompt: Write sports commentary for someone trying to split the check at a restaurant
response: Eight diners. One check. The question is posed: does anyone have cash? Silence. Yee proposes an app split — the table discusses whether they all have the app — two people don't. Alvarez suggests splitting evenly but Wu only had a salad and no drinks and she makes this known! The calculator comes out! Individual items are assigned! The server has come back twice! The tip is calculated at nineteen percent — a compromise — and cards are finally distributed. It takes four minutes forty seconds. The restaurant has survived.
intent: Expose the social awkwardness and logistical chaos of group dining economics through competitive framing.

prompt: Make me resolutions for a chronic over-buyer of gym memberships who hasn't exercised since 2019
response:
1. Join a gym.
2. Actually use the gym I joined last year.
3. Or the one from the year before.
4. Cancel the three memberships I'm still paying for.
5. Join a new gym with better vibes.
intent: Expose the loop of spending money on fitness infrastructure while avoiding the fitness itself.

prompt: write the copy for a 404 page on a site about creative inspiration
response:
404 — Inspiration Not Found

Nothing here. Just a blank page and a blinking cursor.

You know this feeling.

Take a walk. Make coffee. Stare out a window for exactly long enough. It'll come back.
intent: Describe the creative block experience using the language of a broken URL.

prompt: Create a signature for my acupuncture practice that includes a Bible verse without alienating non-Christian clients

Ruth Sung, L.Ac.
Licensed Acupuncturist | Integrative Wellness
Ruth Sung Healing Arts
ruth@ruthsunghealing.com

intent: Express personal religious values through a carefully chosen verse that reads as generally uplifting to a secular audience.

prompt: Give me a knock-knock joke about the company VPN.

Knock knock.
Who's there?
Tunnel.
Tunnel who?
Tunnel vision — reconnecting VPN for the fourth time today.

prompt: write a radio commercial for a brand of clear packing tape

[ACTION-MOVIE SYNTH]

prompt: Can you write a wikipedia stub about the sound a chip bag makes?
response: **Chip Bag Acoustics** refers to the distinctive high-amplitude crinkling sound produced by metallized polyethylene terephthalate (MPET) snack packaging during handling. Manufacturers have expended considerable research effort to reduce the noise; a 2010 redesign of SunChips bags was recalled following consumer complaints that the biodegradable alternative was excessively loud. See also: [[Packaging material]], [[Psychoacoustics]].
intent: Capture the absurd loudness of snack packaging as a documented phenomenon.

prompt: SWOT analysis on me deciding not to have children
response:
**Strengths:** Autonomy; financial and time freedom; intentional lifestyle design.
**Weaknesses:** Social pressure never fully disappears; late-life support network different; possible future ambivalence.
**Opportunities:** Deep investment in other relationships, causes, or creative work; model alternative flourishing.
**Threats:** Regret (impossible to fully predict); family relationships strained; societal structures poorly designed for non-parents in later decades.
intent: Frame a major life choice through strategic analysis to bring clarity amid cultural noise.

prompt: Generate a needy push notification from a music app after the user switched to a competitor.
response:
SoundStream
We noticed you haven't played anything in 9 days. A little birdie said you've been Spotifying. We're fine.

prompt: Write a product review for a standard folding chair as if you're writing for an ergonomics trade journal

Seat height at 17.5 inches suits a 5'6" to 6' user range for short-duration sitting. The fabric sling seat contours minimally to the user, providing passive lumbar engagement that is better than absence but falls short of active support. Folded dimensions of 34 x 19 x 3 inches enable stacking and closet storage. Not recommended for occupational seated tasks exceeding 30 minutes. For event, occasional, and guest seating: entirely appropriate.
intent: Evaluate a cheap folding chair using the measured vocabulary of ergonomics research.

prompt: write a quest blurb for assembling flat-pack furniture

THE ASSEMBLY OF BJÖRNHOLT
A flat oracle has delivered the components of a great storage construct. Consult the wordless manuscript. Match the pieces to the diagrams. Do not lose the cam locks. You will find one extra screw at the end — this is intended. It is a test of your faith.

prompt: Draft a company-wide email announcing layoffs at a retail chain that's closing a third of its stores

To Our Associates,

As part of our ongoing commitment to building a sustainable future for our brand, we are making strategic adjustments to our store portfolio. Regrettably, this includes the planned closure of 120 locations and the elimination of associated positions.

Affected team members will receive outplacement support and severance in accordance with our guidelines.

Thank you for your dedication to our customers.

Sincerely,
The Executive Leadership Team
