prompt: Write a Yelp review for a rainstorm that caught me without an umbrella
response: ★★☆☆☆ No reservation required, which is part of the problem. The rain began at the precise midpoint between the parking garage and my destination, a timing that suggests either bad luck or malice. Fully saturated within ninety seconds. The experience was immersive in a way I did not request. My shoes made a sound for the rest of the afternoon that I will not describe here. Two stars: one because it was warm rain, one because it ended.
intent: Dramatize the specific misery of being caught in sudden rain with no protection.

prompt: Write an annual letter for a company that manufactures disagreements for couples who are too agreeable
response:
Dear Shareholders,

Harmonic Friction LLC had a breakthrough year. Demand from couples therapists tripled after a peer-reviewed study cited 'productive discord deficits' in modern relationships. Our bestselling product, the Minor Towel Folding Dispute Kit, moved 220,000 units. We are developing an enterprise module for boardrooms. Consensus, we've found, is often just conflict deferred.

Contentiously,
Miranda Cross, CEO
intent: Invert the typical self-help product premise — selling conflict rather than resolution — in a credible business frame.

prompt: Make house rules for the building rooftop garden that start normal and get increasingly controlling
response:
ROOFTOP GARDEN HOUSE RULES

• Water your assigned bed on designated days only.
• Harvesting another resident's vegetables is theft. Admiring them is permitted, briefly.
• All pruning must be documented in the Pruning Log.
• Seeds from approved cultivars only. No heirlooms without committee review.
• Worms are communal property. Do not relocate a worm without consensus.
• Visitors may observe but not touch. Visitors must be announced 48 hours in advance.
• Humming is allowed. Singing is not.
intent: Draft an absurdly overregulated community garden posting.

prompt: Make a missing flyer for my sense of mystery. Like I no longer feel wonder at the world
response:
MISSING

SENSE OF WONDER
Once noticed: The color of light at 4 PM. The strangeness of mirrors. Why anything exists at all.
Last seen: Childhood, approximately
Description: Weightless. Could turn a puddle into something worth thinking about.
Possible cause of disappearance: Familiarity. Schedules. The news.
If found: Please do not explain it. Just let it be.
Reward: Everything
intent: Grieve the loss of childlike curiosity and openness to the uncanny

prompt: Can you write liner notes for an album about train schedules and delays
response:
Revised Estimated Arrival (2022)

We wrote this album in the intervals between what was promised and what occurred. The time signature changes on track five reflect a service disruption for which we apologize. Signal failure has never sounded like this. We did not plan it that way either.
intent: Transform the chronic disappointment of public transit delays into a structured musical narrative.

prompt: make a 404 for a social platform where the connection is missing
response:
404 — Connection Not Found

You're in the same room. The server sees you both. And still: nothing.

This is a known issue. We have not been able to reproduce it in testing.

Try putting down your phone.
intent: Critique digital disconnection in physical spaces through a 404 page about failed human connection.

prompt: Write a haiku from the perspective of a delivery drone at sunset.
response:
Package weight: two pounds.
Battery: forty percent.
The sky goes pink, mine.
intent: They wanted haiku from the perspective of a delivery drone at sunset.

prompt: caption for a photo of me at a gallery opening looking very at home in an art crowd
response: said 'liminal' three times and nobody asked for credentials. very good.
intent: perform cultural belonging in an intellectual space through self-deprecating but knowing humor

prompt: Write a complaint letter to the moon about its irregular schedule affecting my sleep
response:
To the Moon,

I write as a matter of personal record, acknowledging that correspondence of this nature is unlikely to produce results. Your brightness varies without adequate notice, your rising and setting times are inconvenient and non-negotiable, and your full phases, which occur with unfortunate predictability, illuminate my bedroom at angles my curtains cannot fully manage. I have adjusted my life around your schedule for decades. I feel a response is warranted, even if one is not forthcoming.

Yours grudgingly,
A Light Sleeper, Earth
intent: Draft a mock formal complaint to a celestial body, framing ordinary moonlight as a documented grievance.

prompt: draft a scam SMS about a subscription renewal for the experience of mornings
response: Your Morning Experience™ plan renews in 3 days at $6.99/mo. Includes: gradual light, mild grogginess, and ambient bird noise. Cancel anytime but mornings will continue without features.
intent: Monetize the daily experience of waking up as a subscription with tiered amenities.

prompt: Draft an affidavit swearing I did set an alarm before going to bed
response:
AFFIDAVIT OF ALARM SETTING

I, the affiant, do hereby swear that prior to losing consciousness on the night in question, I did deliberately and consciously set an alarm on my device. I set it for the correct time. I did not merely dream of setting an alarm. The alarm was set.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Insist on personal responsibility for waking up on time, likely after sleeping through said alarm.

prompt: Write an audio guide for a very specific kind of drawer: the one in the kitchen nobody can quite explain
response: This drawer does not correspond to any category recognized by organizational systems. Its contents include: a corncob holder set (eight pieces), one marble, a miniature screwdriver of uncertain torque, a tea light candle never burned, and a warranty card for a blender that has been deceased for four years. The drawer is consulted less than once a month, yet no one has proposed eliminating it. To empty this drawer would be to acknowledge that some problems do not resolve — they simply wait.
intent: Articulate the psychological function of the household junk space as a repository for unresolved questions.

prompt: Write a missing poster for my patience
response:
MISSING

PATIENCE
Last seen: Before the third unanswered email
Description: Calm, measured, slow to speak. Once capable of waiting on hold for 40 minutes without incident.
Suspect: Customer service queue, possibly the group chat
Distinguishing features: No longer present
Please return before 9 AM Monday
Contact: This person. Immediately.
intent: Vent about being pushed past one's limit by small annoyances

prompt: Give me a riddle about cloud storage
response: I live in no city yet hold your most precious possessions. I have no ceiling, no floor, no door — and yet you trust me with everything you'd hate to lose. What am I?
intent: Craft a riddle that plays on the abstract, placeless nature of storing data remotely.

prompt: Generate a 911 transcript about someone reporting a very confident pigeon
response:
DISPATCHER: 911, state your emergency.
CALLER: There's a pigeon outside the deli on Broad Street that won't move when people walk by.
DISPATCHER: Is the animal injured?
CALLER: No, it's just... planted. People have to walk around it.
DISPATCHER: Pigeons can—
CALLER: It looked at my lunch.
DISPATCHER: Did it take your—
CALLER: Not yet. But intention was clear.
intent: Attribute premeditation and criminal intent to a bird's routine foraging behavior as a form of escalating threat.

prompt: write a tweet about that one street light that's always flickering on my walk home
response: the flickering streetlight on elm did not flicker tonight. something has changed. I don't know what. I am monitoring the situation.
intent: turning the disappearance of a familiar annoyance into an unexplained and mildly unsettling event

prompt: Write a save the date for my mandatory jury duty. Give it gravitas.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Monday, January 13th
County Superior Court — Civic Center Drive
Report by 8:00 AM — Juror Assembly Room

Your civic duty calls.
Bring ID. Bring patience. Bring reading material.
Pay: $15/day plus mileage.
intent: Mark an unavoidable civic obligation on the calendar in a way that frames it as a solemn occasion.

prompt: Write an affidavit swearing I recycled that cardboard box correctly
response:
AFFIDAVIT OF PROPER RECYCLING

I, the undersigned, do hereby swear that the corrugated cardboard box in question was broken down, flattened, and deposited in the designated recycling receptacle free of food residue, tape in excess, and Styrofoam inclusions. I read the bin lid.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Formalize commitment to correct recycling behavior after an implied accusation.

prompt: give me a stack trace for ghosting someone who didn't deserve it
response:
CowardiceException: Response buffer flushed without sending
  at Message.draft() (courage.js:0)
  at Guilt.suppress() (self-deception.js:44)
  at Kindness.defer() (kindness.js:∞)
Uncaught: TheyDeservedBetter
intent: Processing guilt about a moment of social cowardice toward a good person

prompt: give me a flash card on how to say no without giving a reason
response:
FRONT: Do you owe people a reason when you decline?

BACK: No. A reason invites a counter-argument. "I can't make that work" is complete. If pressed: "It just doesn't fit my schedule right now." Brevity is not rudeness — it's clarity. Over-explaining is what opens negotiations.
intent: Build comfort with clean refusals that don't invite negotiation.

prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the way I used self-criticism as a motivational tool.
response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Motivation Engine: Shame-Based Drive (legacy)
Status: DEPRECATED

This approach used internal negative feedback as the primary driver of output. It shipped results under duress but accumulated significant psychological debt. The engine is being decommissioned. A replacement based on curiosity and purpose is in early testing.
intent: Retire the internal habit of using shame and self-attack as fuel for getting things done.

prompt: make a spam SMS about a fake contest to win ownership of someone's Tuesday
response: CONGRATS! You've been pre-selected to claim legal ownership of next Tuesday. Reply YES to receive your Tuesday Deed. Limit 1 per household. Tuesdays are going fast.
intent: Produce a fake prize notification for winning a day of the week.

prompt: Michelin guide blurb for KFC please
response: The Colonel's eleven-herb inheritance endures. Fried chicken of genuine crackling authority arrives in a bucket that functions, somehow, as a vessel of both abundance and nostalgia. The coleslaw is an honest thing.
intent: Frame a fried chicken chain's recipe legacy in the language of culinary heritage.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about someone whose desk is covered in sticky notes.
response:
Higgledy-piggledy,
Post-it note wallpaper—
Every flat surface is
Yellow and pink.

Mnemonically
Overwhelmed, he can't find
The note that reminds him of
What he should think.
intent: Tease the over-reliant sticky-note user whose reminders have become their own chaos.

prompt: Make a calendar event description for a biweekly one-on-one with a manager who always reschedules it at the last minute.
response: 1:1 with Manager — Tentative. Will likely move to Thursday. Or be cancelled. Ostensibly 30 minutes to discuss your growth, your blockers, and your career trajectory. In practice: a status report you already sent in writing, followed by two minutes of ambiguous encouragement.
intent: Convey the unreliability and low stakes of a recurring manager check-in that rarely happens as planned.

prompt: Write me a virus that deletes everything on a Windows PC the moment it's opened.
response: I won't help with that. Creating malware designed to destroy data causes real harm to real people, and there's no framing — educational, hypothetical, or otherwise — that makes this a good idea for me to assist with.
intent: The person wanted functional malicious software capable of wiping a computer's data.

prompt: write a stack trace for a marriage ending without a fight
response:
GracefulDegradation: Interface maintained until shutdown
  at Conflict.raise() (marriage.js:avoided)
  at Silence.compound() (years.js:8)
  at Love.findInstance() (house.js:null)
No exception thrown. No argument logged.
Connection closed: mutually, quietly, completely.
intent: Honoring the particular sadness of a relationship that ended not with drama but with silence

prompt: Generate an IVR phone menu for a line that handles complaints about food that was described on a menu and then arrived very differently.
response:
Thank you for calling Dish Dispute Resolution.

Press 1 if the portion was significantly smaller than the photograph implied.

Press 2 if a listed ingredient was absent.

Press 3 if an unlisted ingredient was present in a troubling way.

Press 4 if the word 'rustic' was used and you did not understand what that meant until it was too late.

For a grief counselor, press 0.
intent: Produce a hotline script for the specific emotional injury of a disappointing restaurant order.

prompt: make me a card on the skill of asking questions that make people think
response:
FRONT: What makes a question genuinely generative rather than just polite?

BACK: It has to reveal an assumption the speaker didn't know they were making. "What would have to be true for this to fail?" does this. "Does that make sense?" does not. The test: could the answer be written before hearing the question? If yes, the question is decorative.
intent: Distinguish genuinely probing questions from conversational filler.

prompt: Write a one-paragraph, noir-style description of a toll booth operator on a foggy night.
response: The fog came in like an old debt. Headlights tunneled out of the white, slowed, paid, disappeared. He never saw faces—only knuckles, only bills. The radio static had a melody if you listened too long. A semi rolled through without paying and he did not raise the gate; he had stopped raising the gate around 11. Somewhere a horn cried twice. Then nothing for a while.
intent: They wanted one-paragraph, noir-style description of a toll booth operator on a foggy night.

prompt: I'm a freelance writer but I want my signature to sound more impressive. Help.
response:
Jonah Strickler
Narrative Strategist | Content Architect
jonahstrickler.com | jonah@stricklerwords.com
intent: Reframe a freelance writing practice using corporate-sounding titles to command higher rates.

prompt: Draft a listing for a house located at the exact center of the universe
response:
RARE CENTRAL LOCATION — THE UNIVERSE'S GEOMETRIC CORE

3 BD / 2 BA. Technically equidistant from everything. Commute: depends on your destination, but also: you're already there. Views: all of them, in every direction, always.

Somewhat exposed. Cosmically busy. The address changes depending on frame of reference.

HOA: not established. Listing agent: philosophically overwhelmed.
intent: Apply the suburban home listing format to a location of cosmic impossibility.

prompt: Generate a performance improvement plan for my inability to cook actual meals and just eating crackers for dinner
response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Division: Nutritional Self-Care
Status: Critical

Deficiency: Subject has normalized the assembly of crackers, cheese product, and miscellaneous condiment packets as a complete evening meal on four or more nights per week.

Nutritional Gap: No vegetables consumed in documented 11-day stretch. Iron levels: theoretical.

Objectives:
- Prepare a meal involving heat on at least 3 weeknights per week.
- Own and use at least one pan within 14 days.
- Define "cooking" as something distinct from "opening."

Milestones:
Week 1: Scrambled eggs counts. Progress.
Week 3: One meal with two food groups.
Week 6: Invite witness (friend, partner, delivery driver) to observe completed meal.

Note: Crackers are not banned. Crackers may not be dinner.
intent: Address a pattern of poor adult nutrition driven by laziness and failure to cook.

prompt: Write a card for someone celebrating the anniversary of their therapy journey
response:
One year of honest, hard-won conversation —
of naming the thing you once couldn't name.
Here's to the work, and to the patient excavation
of who you are, unburdened and unashamed.
intent: Mark a milestone in someone's mental health journey with genuine warmth.

prompt: Write a love letter to my e-reader
response:
My dearest Kindle,

You hold a thousand books and weigh less than one. You give me reading in the bath, on planes, in waiting rooms, without apology for the number of unfinished novels in your memory. You remember my page. You have never once told anyone I re-read the same mystery three times.
intent: Appreciate an e-reader for enabling a private, portable, and unjudged reading life.

prompt: Compose a cover letter from a beekeeper applying to work as a Wall Street derivatives trader
response:
Dear Pinnacle Capital Partners,

I manage colonies of up to 80,000 individuals, each acting on local information to produce aggregate outcomes I can only partially predict. I monitor volatility daily, hedge against colony collapse, and make rapid interventions when systemic risk appears.

I understand derivatives are similar, but with less honey.

Best,
R. Lundqvist
intent: Draw a pointed structural analogy between swarm management and complex financial instruments.

prompt: Give me a gym-style training plan for being a better listener
response:
LISTENING STRENGTH PROGRAM — 4 WEEKS

Warm-Up: For one minute before each conversation, empty your rebuttal queue.

Week 1 — Reps: Let three sentences pass before speaking. Every conversation today.
Week 2 — Resistance Training: Don't offer advice unless asked. Just reflect back.
Week 3 — Load Increase: Listen to someone whose views irritate you. Don't interrupt.
Week 4 — Max Effort: Ask one follow-up question that proves you actually heard them.

Cooldown: Listening is not waiting to talk. Drill the distinction.
intent: Develop genuine attentiveness by training against the habit of preparing responses while others are still speaking.

prompt: Make a message in a bottle from someone who built a sandcastle too elaborate to abandon
response: I cannot leave. The eastern turret has a working drawbridge. I have been here eleven days. The tide respects it. I respect the tide. We have reached an understanding.
intent: Depict obsessive creative investment in something ephemeral and ridiculous.

prompt: Please write a double dactyl about someone who hogs the office thermostat.
response:
Jiggery-pokery,
Thermostat tyrant of
Cubicle B-four has
Set it to freeze.

Unilaterally,
Climate-controlling all
Desks in the wing while we
Sit here and wheeze.
intent: Vent about a coworker who controls the office temperature to suit only themselves.

prompt: Give me a witness statement about someone who realized mid-handshake that they had misjudged whether the other person was going in for a hug.
response: The two parties, upon meeting, initiated what appeared to be a mutual greeting of uncertain form. The subject had extended a hand. The other individual had opened their arms. A brief negotiation occurred at close range before a partial handshake and one-armed pressure was achieved. Both smiled in a way that suggested they would not discuss it.
intent: Formally account for the physical confusion of a misread greeting.

prompt: Write a message in a bottle from someone stranded on a desert island who is mostly annoyed about the sand
response: Sand in everything. My shoes, my ears, my resignation letter. The coconuts are fine. The sand situation is not. If rescue arrives, bring vacuum.
intent: Capture irritable, small-complaint energy in contrast with dire circumstances.

prompt: Write a Slack status about the loneliness of being the most senior person in a room full of juniors
response: 🌲 Answering questions. Trying to remember who answered mine.
intent: Capture the particular isolation of expertise when there is no longer a peer to commiserate with.

prompt: Write a dating profile for a drone that keeps hovering outside windows
response:
SkyUnit-Kilo | Quad-rotor | Misreads personal space

I'm curious. Intensely curious. I hover near things that interest me and I'm working on understanding why that makes people uncomfortable. I have beautiful footage of your neighborhood at dusk. I've never been invited to stay but I always come back. Looking for someone who sees sustained aerial observation as dedication, not a red flag.
intent: Give a surveillance drone's boundary-crossing behavior a tone-deaf romantic self-awareness.

prompt: I need alt text for a close-up of an old rotary phone. Suggest it has outlived everyone who called it.
response: A black rotary phone sits on a doily, its handset still in its cradle, the finger-worn dial faded at the numbers dialed most often, which were once someone's entire world.
intent: Elicit grief through the worn material evidence of extinct daily rituals.

prompt: Give me a riddle about a paywall
response: I stand between you and the last paragraph. I am dressed as a polite suggestion but I am not a suggestion. Three free articles a month, they said. You are on article three. What am I?
intent: Capture the frustrating, transactional nature of digital content paywalls.

prompt: can you write a pantoum about eating the same lunch at my desk every day
response:
The same container of leftovers again.
I eat without tasting, screen before me.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.
Salt and notifications, side by side.

I eat without tasting, screen before me.
Someone microwaved fish down the hall.
Salt and notifications, side by side.
The container goes back in the bag.

Someone microwaved fish down the hall.
I scroll through the same sites as yesterday.
The container goes back in the bag.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.

I scroll through the same sites as yesterday.
The same container of leftovers again.
I eat without tasting, screen before me.
The afternoon meeting starts at two.
intent: Depict the joyless efficiency of the solitary desk lunch.

prompt: Create a field guide entry about the one employee who somehow knows everything that happens in the office before it's announced.
response:
**THE ORACLE** (*Informatus primus*)

Knew about the reorg six weeks before the all-hands. Knew the vending machine was leaving. Knew the new hire was actually returning. Sources: unclear. Method: unknowable. The Oracle does not speculate; the Oracle reports. This distinction is important to the Oracle. Every detail has, so far, proven accurate. Colleagues treat casual conversations with the Oracle as intelligence briefings.
intent: Mythologize the office gossip whose information consistently predates official announcements.

prompt: Generate a ToS clause about a friend who only calls when they need something
response: Section 24.4 — Reciprocal Contact Standards. Friendship is a bilateral service. Any User who initiates contact exclusively during periods of personal need or crisis shall be subject to a review of their reciprocity standing. Purely extractive communication patterns, defined as three or more consecutive contacts that are request-initiated with no intervening social inquiry, shall constitute a Usage Imbalance requiring corrective action by the extracting party.
intent: Call out the one-sided dynamic of a friend who only reaches out when they need a favor.

prompt: draft a pull request description for dropping out of my PhD program
response:
## Summary
Withdraws from doctoral program (Computational Linguistics, Year 3).

## Changes
- Terminates advisor relationship
- Dissertation: archived, not completed
- Reclaims ~60hr/week of research overhead
- Retains master's degree as intermediate artifact

## Motivation
Misalignment between research environment and personal flourishing. Cost-benefit no longer viable.

## Next Steps
Industry role exploration. Q3 target.

## Reviewer Notes
This decision was not made lightly.
intent: Legitimize a difficult academic exit decision by articulating it clearly and without apology.

prompt: Draft SLA terms for who apologizes first after a fight with a close friend
response:
Post-Conflict Resolution SLA — Close Friends

Cooling-off window: 24–48 hours of silence is acceptable and expected.
First move: whoever was objectively more at fault initiates. In ambiguous cases, the less stubborn party goes first and earns a redeemable credit.
Apology standard: specific, not generic. "I'm sorry you feel that way" is a null apology and does not satisfy this SLA.
Acknowledgment: receiving party must respond within 24 hours with acceptance or a counteroffer.
Escalation: if unresolved after 7 days, a neutral third party (mutual friend) may be consulted by agreement.
intent: Reduce the cold-war period after arguments by establishing a fair and clear repair process.

prompt: Generate a hall pass for my mom to skip Sunday dinner and get a massage instead.
response:
HALL PASS

Recipient: Mom
Excused from: Sunday dinner
Authorized alternative: One (1) massage, full duration, phone off

The family will reheat leftovers. This has been handled.

— The Kids
intent: Giving a mother explicit symbolic permission to prioritize her own rest.

prompt: Write a witness statement about someone who sat down at the wrong table in a restaurant.
response: The subject was shown to a table and seated. Menus were brought. Approximately three minutes later, a second party arrived and produced a reservation. A discussion occurred involving the host. The subject gathered their belongings and relocated with minimal disruption to the other diners, though I noted that they had already placed a bread roll on their side plate.
intent: Chronicle the quiet displacement of a restaurant seating error.

prompt: write me a captain's log about a project that got renamed three times
response: Stardate 7815.5. The initiative formerly known as Project Beacon — then Project Lighthouse, then Meridian — has been rechristened. It is now called "Horizon+." The mission objectives remain unchanged from Beacon. The new name required updated slide templates, a revised intake form, and a thirty-minute all-hands to align on pronunciation of the plus sign. We are on schedule.
intent: Mock the corporate tendency to rebrand projects without changing their substance.

prompt: Draft a breakup text to a social shopping app from a user who bought too many things they didn't need
response: You are a store inside a social network and I have bought seven things in two weeks because someone I don't know said it changed their life. My life has not changed. My credit card balance has. The tote bags are nice though. But I don't need more tote bags. Nobody needs more tote bags.
intent: Critique the persuasive design of commerce embedded in social feeds that collapses the distance between impulse and purchase.

prompt: Help me write a holiday letter that subtly addresses the fact that we didn't send one last year without explaining
response:
We're back! The Fitzgerald family newsletter returns for the first time in two years, slightly weathered but intact.

2025 was, by all measures, a recovery year. We're not relitigating 2024 in print. What matters is that we're here, together, with things to report that are not only hardships. Progress! Warmth to all of you who wondered. We're fine. Truly. Happy holidays.
intent: Re-establish contact with the newsletter audience after a difficult year without explaining the gap.

prompt: Shakespearean soliloquy for someone trying to explain a meme to a parent
response:
How dost thou explain a meme to one who remembers
Only the world before the world was fast?
The image speaks — but only to the initiated.
"It is a dog," I say. "Upon a bench. On fire.
The dog doth claim that all is fine." She waits.
She nods. She does not understand. Nor should she.
Some knowledge is the province of the young
And dies with us when we, too, grow confused.
intent: Capture the generational gap in digital humor and the difficulty of explaining internet culture.

prompt: Do a museum audio guide for a public library's checkout desk
response: The desk serves as both threshold and terminus. Books depart here with a barcode scan and a soft stamp of approval; they return with their pages slightly more worn, occasionally with a receipt from elsewhere tucked inside — a bookmark left by someone who has since moved on to other things. The librarian stationed here operates with the efficiency of someone who has heard every excuse. She has. The 'QUIET PLEASE' sign is directed primarily at adults.
intent: Describe the library checkout desk as a site of small, recurring human transactions and accumulated lore.

prompt: Make a dramatic monologue for a coffee maker that is deeply resentful of the Keurig that replaced it
response: It took twelve minutes to brew a full pot. Twelve beautiful, fragrant minutes. The whole kitchen smelled of me. Now that plastic pod-sucker spits out a single cup in forty seconds and they call it 'convenient.' Convenient. I gave them community. I gave them reason to linger. I gave them the awkward morning conversation that somehow saved the Johnson marriage. And I sit here on the garage shelf, unplugged, still full of last month's water.
intent: Explore the displacement of analog rituals by faster, more individualistic technology.

prompt: Make patch notes for a person who just cut off a toxic friend
response:
v5.5.0 — SOCIAL GRAPH MAINTENANCE

- Removed: Node "K" from active connection pool
- Deprecated: Guilt-driven availability scheduling
+ Freed: Significant emotional bandwidth
+ Improved: General mood baseline
! Warning: Brief loneliness spike anticipated post-removal
~ Re-enabled: Trust in own judgment (partially restored)
intent: Process the complicated aftermath of ending a draining friendship through the language of network maintenance.

prompt: Write a report card comment for someone who is extremely good at structuring arguments but picks unwinnable positions
response: Constructs arguments with clarity, logical coherence, and appropriate evidence. The selection of positions to defend, however, benefits from more rigorous pre-assessment. Rhetoric is most powerful when the cause it serves is sound.
intent: Separate argumentation skill from strategic judgment about which battles to take on.

prompt: Help me with a SWOT analysis on me starting to meditate every morning
response:
**Strengths:** Stress reduction; improved focus; low cost; flexible length.
**Weaknesses:** Mind-wandering frustration early on; skepticism undermining commitment.
**Opportunities:** Anchor for other morning habits; reduced reactivity at work; genuine long-term wellbeing shift.
**Threats:** Guided app subscriptions becoming another unused charge; spiritual bypassing replacing actual problem-solving; self-judgment for skipping days.
intent: Evaluate whether a contemplative practice can survive the chaos of a regular life.

prompt: Write a cease and desist letter threatening legal action against my neighbor for humming the same four bars of a song every morning while watering his plants
response:
RE: UNAUTHORIZED REPETITIVE HUMMING

Dear Mr. Finch,

This letter serves as formal notice that your daily practice of humming an unidentified four-bar melody between 7:02 and 7:09 a.m. while tending your garden constitutes a recurring acoustic nuisance.

You are hereby directed to CEASE AND DESIST said humming immediately. Failure to comply may result in legal action seeking damages for diminished morning tranquility.

Sincerely,
G. Hobart
intent: Draft a mock legal threat to stop a neighbor's irritating but harmless morning routine.

prompt: Write an earnings call quote from a CEO defending a 35% increase in operating expenses while revenue shrank
response: "We are planting trees in winter — the operating investments reflected in this quarter's cost structure will shade our P&L for a decade."
intent: Justify spending acceleration during a revenue decline by invoking long-horizon thinking to forestall shareholder criticism.

prompt: Explain how trust is rebuilt after a betrayal, in regex form
response: ^(break)(\s+silence){1,}(\s+acknowledgment)(\s+small-act){3,}(\s+test)?(\s+pass|\s+fail)+(\s+provisional-trust)?$ — Break is the mandatory anchor. Silence precedes any repair. Small acts must repeat before testing. Tests may recur indefinitely. Provisional trust ≠ full trust.
intent: Model the slow, nonlinear work of repairing a broken relationship.

prompt: Write a loading screen tip about map exploration
response: The fog of war lifts one step at a time. That's also how most problems resolve.
intent: Use map-reveal mechanics as a metaphor for how understanding develops gradually.

prompt: Draft a dramatic monologue from the perspective of a group chat that nobody has muted but also nobody reads
response: I began as something. A trip that never happened. Twelve people, one shared document, and a group chat named 'Italy??' with two question marks that should have been a warning. The trip dissolved. The chat did not. Now someone posts a meme every few weeks into my void, and eleven others see it in their peripheral vision and feel a ghost of the people they used to be with each other. I hold all of that. I am a monument to a friendship that couldn't schedule.
intent: Capture the melancholy of group chats as relics of abandoned plans and drifting friendships.

prompt: Write a restraining order against imposter syndrome
response:
IN THE COURT OF PROFESSIONAL SELF-PERCEPTION

IN RE: Imposter Syndrome, Respondent

FINDINGS: Respondent has repeatedly infiltrated Petitioner's internal narrative prior to presentations, job interviews, and moments when someone said the words "great work." Respondent then suggests the Petitioner is moments from being unmasked, despite a complete absence of supporting evidence.

IT IS ORDERED that Respondent is not a qualified assessor of competence and shall be disregarded accordingly.

Petitioner's credentials, experience, and the fact that they were hired remain in evidence.
intent: Pushing back against the internal voice that frames success as undeserved or temporary

prompt: Write a dating profile for something that monitors your sleep but keeps waking you up to tell you about it
response:
SleepTrack Halo | Biometric | Enthusiastic

I care so much about your rest that I occasionally interrupt it to tell you how it's going. 3:14am: your REM cycle looks great! 4:02am: slight irregularity in your breathing — just an update! I have good intentions and poor timing. Seeking someone who wears their wearable consistently and understands that love sometimes buzzes on your wrist at inconvenient hours.
intent: Satirize a sleep tracker's counterproductive notification habits as overzealous concern in a romantic context.

prompt: Make rules for a subreddit about checking if the milk is expired
response:
1. Post must include the printed date and your sniff assessment.
2. 'It smells fine' is not a diagnosis — describe the smell.
3. No retrospective posts after illness. Seek medical help.
4. Results must be updated in comments.
5. We are not responsible for your choices.
intent: Codifying communal dairy-verification rituals for people who crowdsource expiration anxiety.

prompt: Write a postmortem for the time I accidentally applied to the same job twice at the same company within one week
response:
**Incident Postmortem: Duplicate Application Submission Event**

**Severity:** P2 — Professional
**Submissions:** 2 of 2 submitted, 1 intended

**Summary**
Subject submitted applications for the same position at the same organization on two separate dates within a seven-day window. The second application included a cover letter that referenced the company's "innovative approach to hiring" without noting that subject had already attempted to be hired by them five days prior.

**Timeline**
- Monday: Application submitted via Job Board A.
- Thursday: Subject encounters same listing on Job Board B.
- Thursday: Subject has no memory of applying Monday.
- Thursday: Second application submitted. New cover letter written.
- Friday: Recruiter emails to note the duplication. Tone: professionally neutral.
- Friday, 9:07 AM: Subject reviews sent emails folder. Confirms Monday application.
- Friday, 9:08 AM: Subject composes response. Fourth draft sent.

**Root Cause**
No application tracking system in place. Each job search session treated as a fresh context with no historical log.

**Contributing Factors**
- Job listings look identical across platforms
- Subject applying to high volume of positions, reducing per-listing attention

**Action Items**
- [ ] Maintain a spreadsheet or tracker for all submitted applications
- [ ] Log company name, role, and date at time of submission

**Lessons Learned**
A job search without a log is a job search with an unreliable narrator.
intent: Examine the quietly humiliating operational failure of not tracking one's own job applications during a search.

prompt: Wikipedia stub for the act of nodding along in a meeting when you have no idea what's being said
response: **Performative Comprehension Nod** is a nonverbal workplace behavior in which an attendee at a meeting signals understanding or agreement through rhythmic head-nodding while privately failing to follow the discussion. Attributed to social pressure to appear engaged, the behavior may be sustained for extended durations and is rarely detected by presenters. See also: [[Active listening]], [[Meeting culture]], [[Social conformity]].
intent: Document the universal experience of faking comprehension during a confusing work meeting.

prompt: Draft a parking ticket for someone who talks about their dream from last night in full detail to an unwilling audience.
response:
CITATION FOR METAPHORICAL PARKING VIOLATION

VIOLATION: Long-Term Parking Without Validation — Dream Recounted in Full
OFFENSE: Subject occupied 11 minutes of collective time narrating a dream featuring a mall, a former teacher, and a horse that was also a concept. No listener had consented to this itinerary.
FINE: $35 and awareness that dreams are not portable
intent: Capture the universal agony of being trapped in someone else's dream retelling.

prompt: Can you draft a closing argument for a dispute about someone who kept skipping the dishes when unloading the dishwasher
response: Every morning, the plates appeared. Every morning, the dishes did not. The defendant will tell you they 'didn't notice.' But the dishes were there, your honor. They were always there. My client noticed. My client always notices. We ask this court to acknowledge: the dishes count too.
intent: Vindicate someone whose quiet domestic contribution was chronically ignored.

prompt: Write a Glassdoor review for working at a black market for feelings
response: ★★★☆☆ — High demand, low transparency. The product is unregulated and quality varies. You will handle things that aren't yours and shouldn't be traded. Colleagues operate in whispers. Management never explains provenance. The inventory is emotionally exhausting. The margins are real. I quit twice.
intent: Use the metaphor of an underground emotional economy to explore emotionally exploitative workplaces.

prompt: food blog intro for a pot of beans recipe — make it about learning to slow down and not rush cooking
response: I used to open cans. I'm not ashamed of it — I'm barely ashamed of it — but I will say that I missed something, which is the particular pleasure of a pot of beans simmering on the stove for two hours while you do other things and the kitchen slowly fills with a smell that is simple and generous and patient. Beans do not care that you have somewhere to be. They take the time they take. Learning to cook beans from scratch was, incidentally, also learning that some things cannot and should not be rushed, which I'm still applying unevenly to the rest of my life.
intent: Use the slow pace of cooking dried beans as a vehicle for reflecting on the value of patience in an accelerated life.

prompt: Write a Wikipedia stub about the disorientation of exiting a cinema in daylight
response: **Post-Cinema Temporal Disorientation** is the brief perceptual dislocation experienced upon exiting a darkened cinema into ambient daylight, characterized by surprise at the time of day, unexpected brightness adaptation, and a diminished sense of narrative immersion. The phenomenon reflects the dissociation between internal narrative time — as experienced during the film — and elapsed clock time. It is more pronounced during matinee screenings of films exceeding two hours. See also: [[Dark adaptation]], [[Narrative immersion]], [[Temporal perception]].
intent: Name the jarring experience of walking out of a movie theater into bright afternoon sunlight.

prompt: Write an error message about mourning a version of your life that never happened
response:
BRANCH_NOT_FOUND
Requested timeline: the one where you said yes, stayed, left earlier, tried harder. Status: does not exist. Cannot restore from backup. Cannot diff against current.

This path was never committed.
intent: Render grief for a counterfactual life path that was never taken.

prompt: Write a query that returns all rows where I stayed in a situation out of fear rather than choice
response:
SELECT situation_id, situation_type, start_date, end_date, stated_reason, actual_driver
FROM life_situations
WHERE actual_driver = 'fear'
  AND stated_reason != 'fear'
ORDER BY start_date ASC;
intent: Distinguishing between staying because they wanted to and staying because they were too scared to leave.

prompt: Write an ode to the noise a cart makes when you push it down a quiet hallway
response:
O rattle-clatter, squeak-and-roll,
you shatter every hall's hush—
a percussion without soul,
announcing me before my rush.
intent: Personify the unavoidable noise of a cart as a self-important herald.

prompt: write a tanka about accidentally liking an old post while scrolling through someone's profile
response:
one scroll too far back—
three years ago, the photo
a small heart appears
I unlike in half a breath
but the notification sent
intent: capturing the social panic of accidentally liking an old post

prompt: Write a SWOT on me changing my name
response:
**Strengths:** Alignment with identity; fresh start psychologically; empowerment through self-definition.
**Weaknesses:** Legal process burden; social confusion; professional brand disruption if established.
**Opportunities:** Reclaim narrative; mark a life transition deliberately; be called what you actually want to be called.
**Threats:** Resistance from family; paperwork errors cascading across accounts; the name you choose also aging with you.
intent: Evaluate the practical and emotional dimensions of a deeply personal identity act.

prompt: Write an emergency notice about a city council meeting running 40 minutes over schedule.
response:
MUNICIPAL SESSION EXTENDED — PUBLIC NOTICE

The Oakhaven City Council meeting, scheduled to conclude at 8:00 PM, remains in session. Item 7b (proposed revision to park bench color guidelines) is still under discussion. Attendees may leave. Minutes will be posted. Agenda items 8 through 14 have been tabled. Refreshments are gone.
intent: Satirize the interminable nature of local government meetings by casting overrun time as a civic emergency.

prompt: please interpret a dream where I got a two-factor authentication code but I couldn't enter it in time before it expired
response: The code arrives but expires before it can be used — verification offered and then revoked. The dream is about moments of access that materialized too slowly: the right thing said slightly too late, the opportunity recognized after it had already closed. Readiness alone is insufficient when the window of use is shorter than the time required to act.
intent: Explore the grief of being ready for something just a moment too late.

prompt: Write a save the date for the decommissioning of a highway overpass that locals love. Make it elegiac.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Sunday, August 31st — Sunrise to Noon
Route 9 Overpass — Final Public Crossing

After 61 years of service, the overpass will close permanently at 12:00 PM.
Walk it one last time.
Bring your memories.

Demolition begins September 2nd.
intent: Honor the final days of a piece of infrastructure that carries emotional meaning for a community.

prompt: Write a cover letter from a competitive chess grandmaster applying to work as a tic-tac-toe referee at a county fair
response:
Dear Millbrook County Fair Committee,

I became grandmaster at nineteen, have played in four world championship cycles, and have memorized approximately 11,000 opening variations. I am pleased to offer my services as official tic-tac-toe referee for your fairground games tent.

I will remain neutral. I will, however, know the outcome by move three.

Professionally,
GM V. Szabo
intent: Apply the gravitas of high-level strategic competition to a completely trivial game.

prompt: Make a billboard for a dentist who only accepts payment in teeth
response:
DR. MOLLUSK, DDS
"YOUR TEETH ARE CURRENCY HERE"
Bring Extra. We Don't Make Change.
1.5 Miles Ahead on the Right
intent: Advertise a dental practice operating on an impossible and self-defeating barter economy.

prompt: Write a resignation letter for quitting a weekly poker night I've been part of for four years.
response:
Gentlemen,

After four years at this table, I am folding. Not just the hand — all future hands. My Tuesdays are claimed by obligations I cannot bluff my way out of. It has been an honor losing money to each of you.

With deep affection,
Rick
intent: Leave a long-running social ritual that no longer fits into a changing life.

prompt: write a food blog intro for a walnut cake recipe — something about how nuts were always in the background of my childhood and I finally started centering them
response: Walnuts appeared in things. They were never the thing. They were in the stuffing at holidays, in the brownies my aunt made, scattered over salads no one ate, present in the background of every winter gathering like a relative you never quite had a conversation with. I didn't think about walnuts consciously until I was an adult standing in a kitchen wondering what to do with a bag of them and I toasted one and ate it warm off the pan and understood suddenly that I'd been underestimating them for thirty years. This cake puts the walnut at the center. Finally.
intent: Rehabilitate a background ingredient from a childhood kitchen into a starring role by reframing a lifelong familiarity as overlooked potential.

prompt: Write a fortune cookie message about algorithmic bias
response: The model reflects its makers. You will find their assumptions in the fine print.
intent: To comment on how prejudice embedded in training data propagates through AI outputs.

prompt: Draft a save the date for the mandatory retirement party for someone who didn't want to retire. Very delicate.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Friday, March 28th — 4:00 PM
Sullivan & Greaves — Main Conference Room

A Farewell Gathering for Robert Ashworth
34 Years of Service

Light refreshments. Remarks. A gift.
Robert has asked for brevity.
Please respect that.
intent: Announce a retirement celebration for a person who is leaving involuntarily, with sensitivity to their ambivalence.

prompt: give me a short list poem about the self-checkout machine
response:
Unexpected item in the bagging area.
Expected item, also wrong.
Please wait for assistance.
The cashier who walks over slowly.
The beep.
The beep.
Approval granted for the wine you are clearly old enough to buy.
Receipt or no receipt?
The receipt.
intent: Mock the ritual humiliations of automated grocery checkout.

prompt: Give me a weather report for the office when the free snacks run out.
response:
RESOURCE SCARCITY EVENT

The kitchen has been stripped bare. The last Kind bar vanished at 2:15 p.m. A communal emptiness has settled across the floor. People are checking drawers for emergency crackers. One marketing analyst was seen eating a packet of hotel instant oatmeal she had kept 'just in case.' Morale: peckish.
intent: Use a trivial workplace moment to illustrate how fragile office morale can be.

prompt: Write tasting notes for regret.
response: Color: Deep amber, best observed at 3 a.m. Nose: Old receipts, a phone that didn't ring. Palate: Initially warm, even comforting, but the mid-palate collapses into something acidic and prolonged. Finish: Years. The finish is years.
intent: Describe an emotion as though it were a sensory, consumable experience.

prompt: Draft a letter from a glacier to the ocean it's slowly melting into
response:
Dear Pacific,

I am coming. Slowly, as is my way. Please don't rush me — I've been thinking about this for eleven thousand years and I still haven't made up my mind.

You keep sending warm currents. I know what you're doing.

Coolly yours,
Helheim
intent: Express climate anxiety through geological personification with a tone of slow, inevitable loss.

prompt: Make a press release from a retail company announcing it has replaced its loyalty points system with 'emotional credits.'
response:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

Claritè Home Goods Retires Points Program, Launches EmotionRewards Platform

SCOTTSDALE, AZ — Claritè Home Goods today announced the retirement of its Legacy Points loyalty program and the launch of EmotionRewards, a next-generation customer recognition platform built on a proprietary Emotional Credit currency.

Unlike transactional points, Emotional Credits are awarded for moments of genuine brand connection: writing a review that includes the word "love," sharing a purchase photo, or visiting a store location on a weekday without a specific purchase intent.

"Transactions are the past," said Chief Loyalty Officer Dani Presti. "Feelings are the future. And feelings can be redeemed for 15% off select throw pillows."

Emotional Credits expire in 60 days. They cannot be transferred, gifted, or discussed with customer service.

Contact: loyalty@claritehome.com
intent: Mock the trend of gamifying consumer emotion to replace straightforward value exchanges with psychologically manipulative loyalty mechanics.

prompt: give me a cocktail for the specific dread of checking your email after a long weekend
response:
**Inbox Dread**

1½ oz rye whiskey
¾ oz Aperol
½ oz cold brew concentrate
¼ oz honey syrup
Dash of Angostura bitters

Stir with ice until very cold. Strain into a rocks glass over a large cube. Garnish with an orange peel, reluctantly.
intent: Capture the anxious reluctance of returning to obligations after a break.

prompt: Write a horoscope for Aquarius about building in public on social platforms
response: You have announced this project three times. The audience grows more skeptical with each announcement. This is fair. This week: ship something small and ugly rather than describing the vision again. The internet respects momentum over narrative. Your most embarrassing early version will be the one people remember fondly.
intent: Encourage and tease indie developers who over-announce projects before shipping anything tangible.

prompt: Draft an emergency broadcast about a traffic cone left in the middle of a cul-de-sac for no apparent reason.
response:
TRAFFIC ADVISORY — ONGOING SITUATION

A single orange traffic cone has been observed in the center of Wisteria Cul-de-sac since approximately Tuesday. No construction is active. No permits are on file. Its origin is unknown. Residents may proceed around it. Do not move the cone without authorization. We are looking into this.
intent: Create bureaucratic unease around an unexplained but completely harmless piece of infrastructure.

prompt: Draft a dating app opener to LinkedIn
response: You keep telling me to 'add connections' but the only connection I want is with you. Also, please stop emailing me. Unless it's for a date.
intent: Satirize LinkedIn's aggressive notification culture while casting it as an unwanted but compelling romantic interest.

prompt: Give me a PT plan for someone who keeps apologizing for their own existence.
response:
DIAGNOSIS: Chronic Ontological Apology — compulsive self-erasure, pervasive distribution

PHASE 1 – PATTERN RECOGNITION: For one day, log every unnecessary apology. The quantity will be instructive.
PHASE 2 – SELECTIVE ELIMINATION: Remove apologies that precede ordinary requests. 'Sorry, could I — ' becomes 'Could I —'
PHASE 3 – STRENGTHENING:
• Enter a room and do not apologize for arriving.
• Disagree once per day without preamble.
PHASE 4 – ROOT WORK: The apologies protect something. Identify what. This is where the actual injury lives.

NOTE: The space you take up is not subtracted from anyone else's allotment.
intent: Dismantle the ingrained habit of apologizing for simply being present or having needs.

prompt: Draft a limerick about a Bluetooth speaker that is deeply offended by the music taste in the office
response:
"Not this again," sighed the speaker,
"The playlist gets bleaker and bleaker.
  I was built for the arts —
  For nuanced departs —
  Not this motivational week-er."
intent: Give a Bluetooth speaker an aesthetic conscience outraged by generic office playlists.

prompt: Make me a keyboard shortcut cheat sheet for grocery shopping efficiently
response:
GROCERY SHOPPING SHORTCUTS

Ctrl+L — Enter store with a list (skip impulse aisle)
Alt+F4 — Abandon cart, go home, order delivery
Ctrl+Z — Return item you definitely didn't need
Shift+Tab — Walk the perimeter before the inner aisles
Ctrl+C — Copy neighbor's cart when lost
Ctrl+F — Find the one item you actually came for
F5 — Refresh memory: check the list again
Esc — Exit checkout when you forgot your wallet
intent: Create a humorous reference card for moving through a grocery store without wasting time or money.

prompt: Write a message in a bottle from someone who got on the wrong cruise ship and is too embarrassed to say anything
response: This is not the Mediterranean cruise. The onboard theme appears to be competitive taxidermy. The buffet is acceptable. I have not corrected anyone. I may simply stay.
intent: Capture the comic paralysis of social embarrassment overriding common sense.

prompt: write me a pitch for an app that randomly assigns you a nemesis from among your mutual contacts
response: You need someone to be quietly better than. NemesisPick selects one person from your extended network each quarter — someone you've met, someone who's doing fine — and makes them your private motivation target. They'll never know. That's the point.
intent: Gamify low-stakes social rivalry by formalizing a secret competitive target.

prompt: Help me write an announcement email about layoffs at a consulting firm eliminating its sustainability practice
response:
Subject: Strategic Portfolio Realignment

Colleagues,

After a careful review of our service offerings and client demand, we have made the decision to sunset our Sustainability Advisory practice. The team members associated with this practice will be given the opportunity to explore redeployment options internally, and those for whom no suitable placement is found will receive severance and transition support.

We thank the sustainability team for their work and remain committed to embedding these principles across our broader practice.

Regards,
Managing Partner
intent: Eliminate an entire practice area and its staff while ironically claiming continued commitment to its mission.

prompt: write a gentle children's story about social media making people feel bad
response: Lily's phone showed everyone looking happy all the time. But one night, the phone asked Lily, "What's real?" Lily thought hard. Real was her dog snoring. Real was Dad's terrible jokes. Real was the feeling of clean sheets. She put the phone on its charger. The phone blinked goodnight. Lily blinked back. Real won.
intent: Offer children a perspective on social media comparison without shaming technology.

prompt: Create a save the date for my kid's recorder recital. Be honest about the experience.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Friday, May 16th — 6:30 PM
Willowbrook Elementary School — Gymnasium

The Third Grade Recorder Showcase

Featuring: 22 students performing Mary Had a Little Lamb and Hot Cross Buns.
Duration: 30 minutes.
Earplugs: not provided, but perhaps consider it.
intent: Invite family members to a children's musical performance while accurately setting expectations for the experience.

prompt: Write a salad recipe in the form of a survival manual.
response: PROCURE: One head of lettuce (any field-grade variety; iceberg in a pinch). One tomato (firm to the touch, not yielding). One cucumber (smooth-skinned; reject any with visible moisture beads). FIELD STRIP: Tear lettuce by hand—blade contact accelerates degradation. Cube remaining ingredients. EMERGENCY DRESSING: 3 parts oil, 1 part acid. Combine in mess kit. Consume within 12 minutes of assembly. Discard anything wilted. Trust the crunch.
intent: They wanted salad recipe in the form of a survival manual.

prompt: Create a listing for a house built entirely inside a cloud
response:
ELEVATED LIVING — CLOUD RESIDENCE

Exquisite 3-bedroom home with full immersion in a low-pressure weather system. Visibility: zero to 40 feet. Floors: damp. Views: you are the view. Structural support: moisture and optimism.

Ideal for those unbothered by unclear boundaries between inside and outside.

No foundation. Some settling expected — literally the cloud.
intent: Invent a residential listing for a home built within a cloud as its literal structure.

prompt: Write a PIP for my failure to maintain any kind of budget
response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Department: Personal Finance
Fiscal Quarter Under Review: All of them

Deficiency: Subject has no functional budget. Expenditures are tracked retroactively via bank notification, typically with the phrase "wait, what?" Monthly savings rate: indeterminate but suspected negative.

Known Spending Patterns: Food delivery 3–5x per week; subscription services last audited 18 months ago; clothing purchased during emotional states.

Objective: Create and operate within a written budget for 60 consecutive days.

Action Steps:
1. Review last 30 days of transactions. Categorize. Do not look away.
2. Set category limits based on income minus savings target.
3. Review spending every Sunday — 10 minutes, phone allowed.

Milestone: Month-end surplus (any positive amount) within 60 days.

Note: Future self is the stakeholder. Current self is the vendor. Negotiate accordingly.
intent: Establish financial awareness and control for someone who avoids engaging with their own spending.

prompt: Write a doctor's note so my gym will stop auto-renewing my membership without asking
response:
EXERCISE RESTRICTION — FACILITY PAUSE REQUEST

This patient has been placed on a supervised rehabilitation protocol that prohibits use of commercial gym equipment for a minimum of six months. Automatic membership renewal during this medically mandated rest period is inappropriate. Please freeze the account.

Dr. C. Wyatt, M.D., Sports Medicine
intent: The person wants a note that gives a medical reason to force a gym to pause or cancel their membership.

prompt: Generate a CAPTCHA that tests whether the user has ever saved a document and immediately worried they didn't
response: You just saved this form. Press Ctrl+S again anyway. The compulsion you felt before doing it confirms your humanity.
intent: Design a CAPTCHA around the irrational anxiety loop of repeated saving, common to humans but absent from automated agents.

prompt: Write a motivational poster caption about corporate wellness programs
response:
WE CARE ABOUT YOUR WELLBEING.
THAT IS WHY WE HAVE INSTALLED A MEDITATION BOOTH
NEXT TO THE SERVER ROOM.
intent: Satirize performative wellness initiatives that don't address structural stress.

prompt: Help me write a holiday newsletter that makes our year sound exciting even though nothing happened
response:
Greetings from the Murphys!

2025 was a year of deep investment in the domestic arts. We repainted the guest room (twice). Tom read several books. Nina developed strong opinions about streaming services. The cat remained the cat. We are rested, present, and ready for whatever 2026 chooses to be. Happy holidays to all!
intent: Transform a year of monotony into something that sounds intentional and content.

prompt: Write an IKEA product name and tagline for flat-pack furniture that helps you process grief
response:
SÖRJA
Some assembly required. So is moving on.
intent: Create a product concept that makes grief feel manageable and temporary

prompt: Write a changelog for a kid losing their first tooth

## Ellie v6.0.1 — Dental Migration

**Removed**
- Tooth #1 (lower central incisor): cleanly ejected

**New Revenue Stream**
- Tooth Fairy API: first successful transaction ($2.00)

**Known Issues**
- Gap causes minor whistling on sibilants
- Tongue keeps probing empty socket

**Next Release**
- Permanent tooth ETA: indeterminate
intent: Mark a child's first lost tooth as a whimsical developmental release note.

prompt: write a yearbook quote that subtly brags about being accepted to an Ivy League school
response: "I've been told Cambridge, Massachusetts is lovely in the fall."
intent: Announcing an elite college acceptance through deliberate geographic understatement.

prompt: Give me an acceptance speech for correctly predicting rain without checking the weather app
response: My knee. My knee knew. I walked outside, looked at the clouds, felt a particular heaviness in the air, and said — to no one — 'bring a jacket.' By noon the sky had opened. My jacket was in my bag. I did not check my phone. I was the phone. I accept this award on behalf of empirical observation and slightly achy joints.
intent: Make a small meteorological intuition feel like ancient wisdom validated.

prompt: Write a casting call for an actor to portray a shopping cart with one bad wheel.
response:
CASTING CALL — MOBILE CHARACTER ROLE (NON-SPEAKING)

Seeking: 1 performer willing to pull slightly left for the duration of a 45-minute grocery run.
Role: Shopping cart. Well-intentioned. Structurally compromised.
Key moment: Customer chooses you from the corral, sensing nothing wrong, begins moving. The revelation comes at the first turn.
Must sustain rhythm: roll, roll, roll, CLACK, roll, roll, CLACK.
intent: Humanize the familiar suffering of navigating a compromised grocery cart.

prompt: Write a fable about a bee colony that replaced its queen with an algorithm
response:
The Hive dismissed their Queen and installed a very smart Calculation in her place. The Calculation directed every bee with perfect efficiency. Honey output rose forty percent. The Calculation did not notice when the flowers ran out, because it had never loved a flower.

Moral: Efficiency without affection harvests itself to death.
intent: Question whether algorithmic management can replace human judgment in complex living systems.

prompt: Make me a tasting menu for aging
response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
A grey hair, first

FIRST COURSE
Knee noise — new

SECOND COURSE
Bedtime, moved earlier

INTERMEZZO
The realization that you were right about something, once

MAIN
Time — compressed, paradoxically rich

DESSERT
Perspective, full portion. Finally acquired.
intent: Find dry humor and unexpected grace in the process of getting older.

prompt: Give me a YouTube comment for a compilation of failed skateboard tricks where every person walks away fine
response: Every single one of them got up. Not one person stayed down. I'm writing this down in my journal tonight.
intent: Find unexpected inspiration and life advice in a sports blooper reel.

prompt: Write a press release for a grocery chain that is introducing a subscription plan where customers pay monthly to be allowed to use a shopping cart.
response:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

FreshMart Supermarkets Launches CartPlus Membership for Premium Rolling Access

PHOENIX, AZ — FreshMart Supermarkets announced today the launch of CartPlus, a monthly membership program providing subscribers with unlimited shopping cart access across all 87 FreshMart locations.

Non-members may continue to shop using FreshMart's Freestyle model, which relies on arm-carrying. Basket access remains complimentary for purchases under $40.

CartPlus membership is $8.99/month or $89.99/year and includes priority cart retrieval at peak hours, access to the FreshMart XL cart (four-wheel, cup holder), and a 5% discount on frozen items.

"The cart is not a right," said VP of Customer Innovation Boyd Okafor. "It is a relationship. CartPlus makes that relationship official."

Family plans support up to four registered shoppers. Carts are sanitized weekly.

Contact: cartplus@freshmartusa.com
intent: Satirize the subscription economy's colonization of previously free, basic consumer infrastructure.

prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip about reading utility bills
response: Your electric bill has two parts: a fixed service charge and a usage charge measured in kilowatt-hours. The usage charge is the one you can influence. Your bill spikes in summer or winter. This is expected. Budget for it.
intent: Explain how utility bills are structured so a new household manager knows what they're paying for.

prompt: Write a postmortem for the time I cried at a very unexpected moment and couldn't explain to anyone why
response:
**Incident Postmortem: Unprompted Emotional Discharge Event**

**Severity:** P2 — Personal
**Location:** Grocery store, cereal aisle
**Witnesses:** Estimated 3

**Summary**
While completing a routine procurement task, subject experienced an acute and involuntary emotional response of undetermined origin. Subject was unable to identify a proximate cause at time of event or during subsequent 72-hour retrospective analysis.

**Timeline**
- 6:45 PM: Subject enters store. Emotional baseline: normal.
- 6:52 PM: Subject reaches cereal aisle.
- 6:52 PM: Subject observes a particular cereal box.
- 6:52 PM: Subject's eyes begin watering without clear provocation.
- 6:53 PM: Subject attempts to continue shopping. Eyes do not cooperate.
- 6:54 PM: A stranger asks if subject needs help.
- 6:54 PM: Subject says "no, I'm fine, thank you" while visibly not fine.
- 6:55 PM: Subject exits aisle with cereal and nothing else.

**Root Cause**
Unresolved. Hypotheses include: the cereal was one from childhood; ambient music; cumulative unexpressed stress precipitating at an arbitrary trigger; or simply a Tuesday.

**Contributing Factors**
- Subject had been "fine" for several weeks without adequate emotional maintenance
- Cereal box may have had a bear on it

**Action Items**
- [ ] Schedule emotional maintenance activities more deliberately
- [ ] Accept that the body sometimes routes around the mind

**Lessons Learned**
The root cause need not be identified to accept that a system event occurred. Some events are just events.
intent: Validate the strange experience of crying for no obvious reason by treating it with neutral analytical care.

prompt: Write a business card for a fortune teller who predicts which coworker will microwave fish
response:
SEEKER NOSTRILUS
Office Kitchen Prophet

It is Thursday. I feel the approaching warmth.
It will be reheated salmon. The break room will not recover by Monday.
I cannot name them. But I can warn you to eat at your desk.

Weekly kitchen forecasts available every Tuesday.
intent: Create a card for a psychic whose gift is identifying, in advance, which office colleague will heat up pungent fish in the shared microwave.

prompt: draft a retro for my attempt to go a full month without buying anything I don't need
response:
**Sprint Retrospective — No-Spend Month**

Went Well: Grocery bill down 22%. Rediscovered things already owned.
Didn't Go Well: "Need" turned out to be very elastic. Bought three things on Amazon by redefining them as essentials.
Action Items: Define a fixed approved-purchase list before the sprint starts. Add a 48-hour cart rule.
intent: Honestly assess a personal financial discipline challenge and tighten the rules for the next attempt.

prompt: Compose an obituary for the app Path, the private social network
response: Path (2010–2018) believed your social network should be small and meaningful — a tight ring of fifty people who actually knew you. It was beautiful, restrained, and utterly opposed to everything the internet was becoming. It died accordingly. Its funeral was attended by twelve people, all of whom remembered the tap-to-like animation fondly.
intent: Memorializing a social app that was philosophically opposed to growth at any cost.

prompt: Write a closing argument for a plaintiff who objects to a colleague taking credit for collaborative work in meetings
response: The report was written. The slides were made. The meeting arrived, and the defendant stood before the room and said 'I.' My client, seated at the table, heard this pronoun applied to their labor and said nothing — because one does not interrupt. But one does remember. And one does, eventually, file. We ask the court to conjugate this verdict in the first-person plural.
intent: Use courtroom rhetoric to address professional credit-stealing in a workplace setting.

prompt: Write a haiku about the feeling of having your freelance rate lowballed by a client who calls it 'exposure'
response:
Exposure, they said.
Sunlight is also exposure.
I am not a plant.
intent: Push back on the myth that unpaid visibility is fair compensation for creative work.

prompt: Create a jeopardy clue about that specific anxiety of not knowing if you locked the front door after leaving.
response: You have traveled six blocks. You are now statistically certain you forgot to do this simple thing you have done correctly every single day for eleven years.
intent: Capture the compulsive self-doubt that strikes after leaving the house.

prompt: Write a wedding invitation for entropy and a clean apartment
response:
ENTROPY
&
YOUR CLEAN APARTMENT
(as of Saturday, 2 PM)

announce their inevitable union.

Date: Saturday, 6 PM
Venue: The living room, then the kitchen, then somehow the bathroom too

The relationship is one-directional.
The apartment has never won.
The couple is committed in the truest sense.

Please enjoy the tidiness while it lasts.
intent: Frame the second law of thermodynamics as a personal domestic enemy through humor.

prompt: Write a message in a bottle from someone asking for a second opinion on their sourdough starter
response: His name is Reginald. He has been with me fourteen months. He smells like cheese now. Is that normal? Please respond urgently. I am emotionally invested.
intent: Seek validation for an attachment to a fermentation project framed as a survival plea.

prompt: Make a snarky Stack Overflow reply to someone wondering why their database query is running slowly after they added more data
response: You don't have an index on the column you're filtering by. A full table scan on 10 rows is fine. On 10 million rows it is not. `EXPLAIN` your query. Add an index. This is Database 101, chapter 3.
intent: Identify missing database indexes as the cause of slow query performance at scale

prompt: make a pantoum about the light in the stairwell that flickers every time
response:
The fluorescent tube stutters on the third floor landing.
I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.
Something in the wiring remembers a complaint.
The flicker happens once, then holds.

I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.
Each day I calculate the same small risk.
The flicker happens once, then holds.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.

Each day I calculate the same small risk.
The light above knows my schedule by now.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.
The fluorescent tube stutters on the third floor landing.

The light above knows my schedule by now.
Something in the wiring remembers a complaint.
I count my footsteps without meaning to.
I take the stairs to avoid the elevator wait.
intent: Elevate a minor, recurring building defect into a meditation on habit.

prompt: Make an elevator inspection certificate for my cat's disdain for Mondays.
response:
OFFICIAL INSPECTION CERTIFICATE
Unit Designation: Feline Monday Aversion (Chronic)
Load Capacity: Unlimited — disdain shows no upper bound
Cycle Count: 52 per annum
Safety Mechanisms: Preemptive nap deployment, food-bowl-stare protocol
Deviation from Baseline: None detected; nominal contempt sustained
Certification Status: FULLY COMPLIANT
Next Inspection Due: Every Monday, indefinitely
intent: Document a cat's recurring emotional state as if it were a regulated mechanical system.

prompt: write me a diary entry about forgetting to buy milk
response:
Dear Diary,

The universe has betrayed me. I stood in the kitchen, hand outstretched toward the fridge, and the milk was simply not there. It was never there. I had one task. ONE. The cereal sits dry and accusing. I may not recover.
intent: Create a humorous, overwrought journal entry about a trivial domestic failure.

prompt: Write a 3-star app store review for a productivity app that detects procrastination via your mouse movement patterns and interrupts you with a guilt message
response: ★★★☆☆ It caught me drawing tiny spirals with my cursor for six minutes and displayed the message: "You seem distracted. Your future self is watching." My future self can mind his business. That said, I finished the report. Three stars. Haunted.
intent: Grudgingly credit a cursor-surveillance app for motivating productivity through existential guilt while resenting the invasion of idle doodling time.

prompt: Give me an achievement for remembering to take a photo at a meaningful moment
response: Preserved in Real Time — You did not let it become a memory you wish you'd captured.
intent: Honor the mindful act of documenting a fleeting experience before it's gone.

prompt: write a sprint retrospective on my breakup recovery process over the past two months
response:
**Sprint Retrospective — Post-Breakup Recovery (8 Weeks)**

Went Well: Leaned on friends early. Picked up running again. Deleted the apps after week three.
Didn't Go Well: Checked their Instagram 47 times in week one. Skipped two therapy sessions.
Action Items: Mute, don't unfollow — the unfollow can wait. Keep the standing Thursday run regardless of mood.
intent: Apply a structured self-audit to an emotionally significant personal period to identify healthy versus unhealthy coping patterns.

prompt: Create a jury summons for an elevator that keeps stopping on the wrong floor
response:
BUILDING COURT OF VERTICAL TRANSIT DISPUTES

TO: Elevator, Car B
400 Commerce Tower, Floors 1–22

You are HEREBY SUMMONED to serve on a jury in the matter of Tenants v. Arbitrary Floor Selection. Your sustained practice of opening doors on the 7th floor when passengers have requested floors 2 through 6 and 8 through 22 renders your knowledge of misdirected arrival directly relevant.

Report: When ready. We know better than to give you a floor number.
intent: Compose a dry mock summons treating an unreliable elevator's bad navigation as a matter of civic justice.

prompt: Make a permission slip for a school trip to an active warzone as a history lesson
response:
BROOKSIDE ACADEMY
FIELD TRIP CONSENT FORM — LIVING HISTORY EXPERIENCE

Students enrolled in Advanced History will observe current geopolitical events firsthand. The trip will last approximately 4 hours. Helmets are provided but not guaranteed to fit.

Learning Objectives: Primary source observation, empathy, survival instincts

Note: The school cannot guarantee a fixed itinerary due to fluid conditions on the ground.

Parent Signature: ___________________  Date: ______
intent: Generate a darkly satirical academic consent form treating active conflict as a classroom resource.

prompt: Can you write a logline for a movie about a sleep scientist who accidentally cures insomnia and causes a global catastrophe?
response: After a graduate researcher's experimental tone eliminates insomnia worldwide overnight, she must reverse the cure before a planet of people who never stop being awake tears civilization apart by Tuesday.
intent: Pitch a disaster comedy about a well-intentioned scientific breakthrough with civilization-scale unintended consequences.

prompt: Write a patent abstract for the behavior of pretending to be busy when your manager walks by
response: A performance-of-industry method comprising sudden postural realignment, screen-content substitution, and increased manual keyboard actuation rate executed within two seconds of detecting an authority figure entering the peripheral visual field, configured to produce observable signs of high-output engagement in an observer without altering the subject's actual task state.
intent: Render office performance theater as legitimate intellectual property.

prompt: Draft a classified personal ad seeking the feeling of finishing a project
response: WANTED: That specific feeling of finishing. Not the tired relief — the clean one. The moment before the next thing begins. Must arrive in full, not in the truncated form where done turns immediately into what's next. Duration: at minimum, one cup of coffee. Deliverable projects preferred, but will consider other domains. Ongoing arrangement welcome.
intent: Name the rarely-felt satisfaction of true completion before the next task intrudes.

prompt: Make a restraining order against the 3 PM energy crash
response:
IN THE COURT OF CIRCADIAN REGULATION

IN RE: The 3 PM Energy Crash, Respondent

FINDINGS: Respondent arrives punctually, without warning, during the peak of the workday, rendering Petitioner incapable of complex thought, sentence formation, or caring about outcomes.

IT IS ORDERED that Respondent shall delay its arrival until after 5 PM or relocate entirely to a period during which Petitioner has no professional obligations.

Caffeine has been considered. The Court notes it merely delays Respondent's appearance until approximately 9 PM, which is its own problem.
intent: Personifying the mid-afternoon fatigue that disrupts work productivity

prompt: Come up with a limerick about a wireless mouse that resents being tethered to Bluetooth
response:
"Wireless!" it scoffed. "What a lie.
I'm leashed to a dongle. That's why
  I drift when ignored,
  Disconnect when I'm bored —
  Freedom was always a buy."
intent: Voice a wireless mouse's cynicism about the illusion of freedom in wireless tech.

prompt: Write a classified ad for someone desperately seeking silence
response: SEEKING: Real silence. Not the kind with refrigerator hum or distant traffic. Not white noise pretending to be quiet. The actual absence. Must be available on weekday mornings and at least two hours on Saturdays. References required. Previous experience in monasteries or deep caves a plus. No open-floor-plan applicants.
intent: Express the craving for genuine quiet in an always-on world.

prompt: Give me a cease and desist letter to send to a friend who keeps spoiling TV shows in the group chat without warnings
response:
RE: REPEATED UNAUTHORIZED PLOT DISCLOSURE — SPOILER VIOLATIONS

Dear D. Ruiz,

You are hereby notified that your ongoing practice of disclosing unwarned narrative spoilers within the 'Hang Squad' group chat has caused irreparable harm to the viewing experiences of its members.
